title: Bionano Genomics Reports Strong Q3 2025 Results
summary: Bionano Genomics reported strong Q3 2025 results, with a 21% increase in total revenue year-over-year, reaching $7.4 million. The company, specializing in optical genome mapping, also saw significant improvements in gross margins, which reached 46% from a negative margin the previous year, and a 66% reduction in operating expenses. Bionano Genomics maintained its full-year 2025 revenue guidance of $26.0 to $30.0 million, expecting to exceed prior expectations for new OGM system installations.

title: RBC Bearings director Kaplan sells shares worth $310,100
summary: RBC Bearings director Steven H. Kaplan sold 700 shares of common stock for $310,100, while also exercising options to acquire 200 shares. The sales occurred near the stock's 52-week high, with analysis suggesting the stock is overvalued and in overbought territory. Despite this, RBC Bearings recently reported strong financial results, exceeding earnings and revenue expectations, leading to raised price targets and an upgrade from analysts.

title: St. Louis Boeing machinists approve contract, ending 15-week strike
summary: St. Louis Boeing machinists have approved a new five-year contract with a 68% to 32% vote, ending a 15-week strike that was the longest in the company's St. Louis history. The agreement includes a $6,000 ratification bonus and an average base pay increase from $75,000 to $109,000 annually, with a focus on upfront cash over previous stock options. Although many workers felt the contract wasn't ideal, the decision was driven by the financial strain of the prolonged strike.

title: Boeing strike in St. Louis ends after union approves new contract
summary: A three-month strike by union mechanics at Boeing Defense in St. Louis has ended after members of the International Association of Machinists and Aerospace Workers approved a new five-year contract. The approved offer includes a $6,000 signing bonus and a 24% general wage increase over the contract's term. The agreement comes after contentious negotiations and concerns raised by U.S. senators about the use of replacement workers.

title: Ready, set, Italia! Alaska Airlines opens ticket sales for new nonstop flights between Seattle and Rome on widebody 787 Dreamliner aircraft
summary: Alaska Airlines has opened ticket sales for new daily seasonal nonstop flights between Seattle and Rome, Italy, commencing April 28, 2026. The service will utilize widebody 787 Dreamliner aircraft, offering a reimagined global travel experience with lie-flat seats, enclosed suites in Business Class, and multi-course dining. Roundtrip airfares to Rome are available for as low as $599, and ticket sales for new year-round service between Seattle and London Heathrow will also begin soon.

title: CPI Aerostructures Reports Third Quarter and Nine Month 2025 Results
summary: CPI Aerostructures announced its financial results for Q3 and nine months ended September 30, 2025. The company reported improved Q3 performance with increased net income and adjusted EBITDA compared to Q3 2024, despite nine-month results being impacted by the Boeing A-10 Program termination. CPI Aero also strengthened its balance sheet and secured a new award from Raytheon for structural missile wing assemblies, adding to its $509 million backlog.

title: Southwest Airlines Co. $LUV Shares Sold by M&G PLC
summary: M&G PLC significantly reduced its stake in Southwest Airlines Co. by 98.3% during the second quarter, selling over 24 million shares. Despite this large sale, other institutional investors have shown mixed activity, with some increasing and others decreasing their holdings. The company's board also approved a $2.00 billion share buyback program, signaling confidence in the stock's valuation.

title: AIRO Reports Third Quarter 2025 Results; Progress on Drone Deliveries and Strong Liquidity Position
summary: AIRO Group Holdings, Inc. announced its Q3 2025 financial results, reporting $6.3 million in revenue with approximately $20 million in drone shipments shifted to Q4 2025. The company highlighted significant operational advancements, including a joint venture with Nord Drone Group, a $4.5 million award for Counter Electronic Warfare development, and plans for U.S. manufacturing expansion. AIRO also completed an $89.4 million public offering, strengthening its liquidity to fund growth initiatives in advanced aerospace and defense technologies.

title: FAA grounds additional Boeing models for inspection after UPS crash
summary: The FAA expanded its emergency airworthiness directive to include McDonnell Douglas DC-10 aircraft for inspection, following a fatal UPS cargo plane crash in Kentucky. This directive grounds the planes until inspections are completed, though it is unlikely to impact day-to-day travel as many DC-10s are already retired. The crash, which killed 14 people, involved an MD-11 whose left engine detached during takeoff, prompting concerns about similar unsafe conditions in other Boeing models.

title: FAA grounds additional Boeing models for inspection after UPS crash
summary: The FAA has expanded its emergency airworthiness directive to include McDonnell Douglas DC-10 aircraft for inspection, following a fatal UPS cargo plane crash in Kentucky. This directive was issued due to concerns that these older models might share the same unsafe conditions as the previously grounded MD-11s. The crash, which killed 14 people, involved an MD-11 where an engine detached during takeoff, prompting an investigation into the cause and pilot actions.

title: FAA grounds additional Boeing models for inspection after UPS crash
summary: The FAA has expanded its emergency airworthiness directive to include McDonnell Douglas DC-10 aircraft for inspection, following a fatal UPS crash involving an MD-11. This directive mandates inspections before the planes can be flown, though it is unlikely to affect day-to-day travel as most DC-10s are retired and both UPS and FedEx had already grounded their MD-11 fleets. The crash in Louisville killed 14 people, and investigators are still determining the cause, focusing on an engine detachment and subsequent fire during takeoff.

title: Boeing targets industrial stability before new output rises
summary: Boeing aims to stabilize aircraft production at current levels before increasing output, prioritizing safety and quality improvements after a past incident. Boeing Commercial Airplanes CEO Stephanie Pope stated that the company will only raise production rates for models like the 737 and 787 when its industrial system is fully ready. The company is also setting up a new production line at its Everett factory to accommodate future higher production levels, aiming to meet market and customer demand long-term.

title: Boeing takes cautious path on production increases ahead of 2026 push: report (BA:NYSE)
summary: Boeing plans to hold production steady to prepare its factories for higher output in 2026, indicating a cautious approach to rebuilding manufacturing discipline. This strategy aims to stabilize operations before increasing production rates, impacting short-term growth while addressing past delays and supply chain challenges. The company is focusing on operational health, which contrasts with Airbus's approach despite engine supply bottlenecks in the industry.

title: MJP Associates Inc. ADV Makes New $307,000 Investment in The Boeing Company $BA
summary: MJP Associates Inc. ADV initiated a new $307,000 investment in The Boeing Company, acquiring 1,467 shares in Q2. This comes as analysts have issued mostly positive ratings with an average target price of $240.30 despite Boeing's recent earnings miss. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Boeing, and insider trading activity included both acquisitions and sales.

title: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. MA Sells 77,159 Shares of Northrop Grumman Corporation $NOC
summary: Massachusetts Financial Services Co. trimmed its stake in Northrop Grumman Corporation ($NOC) by 4.0%, selling 77,159 shares and retaining 1,858,575 shares valued at approximately $929.25 million. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target of $642.73, and Northrop Grumman recently exceeded EPS estimates while announcing a quarterly dividend of $2.31.

title: Boeing says partnership for T-7 trainer export bid imminent
summary: Boeing is nearing an announcement for a partnership to bid on international sales opportunities for its T-7 trainer, indicating growing demand for lightweight military training jets. The planned cooperation, expected within the next week, could address Britain's need to replace its Hawk trainer fleet and targets major markets in Europe and Asia. Boeing and Saab jointly developed the T-7 for the U.S. Air Force, while BAE Systems previously partnered with Saab on the Hawk replacement but has not decided on developing a new jet.

title: SG Americas Securities LLC Grows Stock Position in Howmet Aerospace Inc. $HWM
summary: SG Americas Securities LLC significantly increased its stake in Howmet Aerospace Inc. by 55.6% during the second quarter. This move, along with actions by other institutional investors, brings institutional ownership of HWM to 90.46%. The article highlights Howmet Aerospace's strong quarterly earnings, positive revenue growth, and optimistic FY2025 EPS guidance, alongside a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts.

title: Honeywell's Black Box in the Sky Goes Beyond Today's Flight Data Recorders
summary: Honeywell is developing a new generation flight data recorder concept called "Black Box in the Sky," aiming to enhance flight data capture beyond current capabilities. This initiative also seeks to address critical aviation challenges such as GPS jamming and pilot fatigue. The system is designed to provide more comprehensive data and solutions for modern flight concerns.

title: Tempus AI Reports Strong Q3 2025 Growth
summary: Tempus AI (TEM) announced strong Q3 2025 earnings, with revenues up 84.7% year-over-year to $334.2 million, driven significantly by its Genomics business. The company also achieved a positive Adjusted EBITDA of $1.5 million and expanded its capabilities through strategic acquisitions and partnerships. Tempus AI projects approximately $1.265 billion in revenue by the end of 2025, fueled by continued investments in AI and precision medicine.

title: Inside GE Aerospace’s Mission To Build The World’s Toughest Jet Engines
summary: GE Aerospace is heavily investing in engineering, testing, and analytics to produce the most durable jet engines for both commercial and military applications. This initiative focuses on enhancing existing engine families like GEnx, LEAP, and GE9X with advanced materials and rigorous testing, aiming to significantly extend time-on-wing for airlines and reduce maintenance disruptions. The strategy not only addresses airline demands for increased durability but also positions GE as a leader in future ultra-efficient and reliable aircraft engine development.

title: Delta says it saw "zero cancellations" as federal shutdown disruption ends
summary: Delta Air Lines reported "zero cancellations" over the weekend despite widespread concerns regarding potential travel disruptions from a federal government shutdown. The airline's operations remained normal even as federal officials had warned of possible staffing shortages and delayed inspections. It remains unclear how other airlines were affected, as travelers are advised to check for real-time updates.

title: Collins Aerospace (NYSE: RTX) expands Ascentia analytics to nearly 40% of Boeing 787s
summary: Collins Aerospace, an RTX business, has been selected by Qatar Airways to deploy its Ascentia analytics solution across Qatar Airways' fleet of 52 Boeing 787 aircraft. Announced at the Dubai Air Show, Ascentia services aim to transform fleet maintenance by improving reliability, reducing costs, and providing smoother travel experiences for passengers by generating predictive insights into component health based on real-time sensor data and historical records. Collins states that Ascentia currently supports nearly 40% of 787s in operation today.

title: Emirates Boosts Boeing (NYSE: BA) 777X Backlog to 270 Jets with New Dubai Deal
summary: Emirates ordered an additional 65 Boeing 777-9 aircraft at the Dubai Airshow, increasing its total 777X backlog to 270 jets and solidifying its position as the largest 777X customer. This expansion aims to fuel future growth and meet rising travel demand, with the 777-9 expected to offer 20% reduced fuel use and emissions. Boeing's 2025 Commercial Market Outlook projects significant demand for widebody jets in the Middle East over the next two decades.

title: Embraer Will "Take The Time It Needs" To Decide On Building An Airbus & Boeing Competitor
summary: Embraer is deliberating whether to develop a larger aircraft that could compete with the Airbus A320 and Boeing 737, but is in no hurry due to the potentially colossal costs involved. President and CEO of Embraer Commercial, Arjan Meijer, indicated that such a project could cost tens of billions and impact current profitable operations and backlogs. The company also recently shelved plans for a next-generation turboprop, focusing instead on its successful E-Jet series and new orders for its E175 and E195-E2 models.

title: Emirates orders 65 more Boeing 777X jets (BA:NYSE)
summary: Emirates has announced an order for 65 Boeing 777-9 aircraft at the Dubai Air Show. The Boeing 777X is the latest series of long-range, wide-body, twin-engine jetliners from Boeing Commercial Airplanes, providing a significant boost to Boeing's order book.

title: Intuitive Machines to Acquire Lanteris Space Systems, Launching the Next-Generation Space Prime
summary: Intuitive Machines announced its acquisition of Lanteris Space Systems for approximately US$800 million, comprising cash and stock, in a move to become a vertically integrated next-generation space prime. This acquisition will expand Intuitive Machines' capabilities across various orbits and deep space, leveraging Lanteris's expertise in spacecraft manufacturing. The combined entity boasts significant revenue and backlog, positioning it for involvement in multi-billion-dollar space programs including national-security, Artemis lunar, and Mars initiatives.

title: Qatar Airways taps RTX's Collins Aerospace to optimize 787 fleet health and maintenance
summary: Qatar Airways has selected Collins Aerospace, an RTX business, to implement its Ascentia analytics solution across its fleet of 52 Boeing 787 aircraft. This partnership aims to enhance fleet health and maintenance by improving reliability, reducing costs, and ensuring smoother passenger travel experiences. The Ascentia system utilizes aircraft sensor data and advanced statistical models to provide predictive insights, helping airlines reduce unscheduled maintenance and optimize operational efficiency.

title: Bank of Taiwan, Chunghwa Telecom, and Fullon Hotels Are Among the Honoured Winners of the 2025-2026 World Branding Awards in Osaka
summary: The 20th World Branding Awards, held for the first time outside London in Osaka, celebrated leading brands across National, Regional, and Global categories for 2025-2026. Out of over 1,092 nominated brands from 66 countries, fewer than 100 were recognized for their excellence, including global winners like British Airways and IKEA, and Taiwanese winners Bank of Taiwan, Chunghwa Telecom, and Fullon Hotels. The awards highlighted brands demonstrating unwavering effort, vision, and emotional resonance, based on nominations from over 91,000 consumers worldwide.

title: Ethiopian Airlines to Add 11 Boeing 737-8 MAX in Deal with Boeing (NYSE: BA)
summary: Ethiopian Airlines has committed to purchase 11 additional Boeing 737-8 MAX aircraft, solidifying its position as Africa's largest Boeing fleet operator and backlog holder. This agreement, signed at the Dubai Airshow, aims to expand the airline's regional and international routes and grow its Addis Ababa hub. The deal further strengthens an almost 80-year partnership between Boeing and Ethiopian Airlines.

title: Boeing (NYSE: BA) to Supply Air Senegal Nine 737-8s in Its Largest Fleet Purchase
summary: Air Senegal has committed to purchasing nine 737 MAX airplanes from Boeing, marking its largest fleet order and first Boeing purchase since 2004. This acquisition aims to modernize and expand Air Senegal's regional and intercontinental network, leveraging the 737-8's efficiency, range, and reduced environmental impact. The deal supports Boeing's outlook for rising air travel demand in Africa, particularly for single-aisle jets.

title: Boeing’s 777X has had its problems, but the orders keep coming
summary: Boeing received a significant order from Dubai-based Emirates for 65 of its 777X twin-engine aircraft, along with 130 engines from GE Aerospace. This order comes despite recent certification delays that led Boeing to book a $4.9 billion loss on the program. The news caused Boeing's shares to rebound from a six-month low.

title: Ethiopian Airlines adds 11 Boeing 737 Max jets in latest fleet expansion (BA:NYSE)
summary: Ethiopian Airlines has agreed to purchase 11 additional Boeing 737 Max aircraft, expanding its fleet and order book for the single-aisle jet. This commitment, signed at the Dubai Airshow, aims to bolster its regional and international networks.

title: New Collaboration Between MTC and HP Supports UK Additive Manufacturing Growth
summary: The Manufacturing Technology Centre (MTC) has partnered with HP Inc. as a new Tier 2 member to advance Metal Binder Jetting technology in the UK. This collaboration aims to foster industry and academic cooperation, making scalable and cost-effective additive manufacturing solutions more accessible. The partnership aligns with broader UK government initiatives to support additive manufacturing, including significant investments in defence and aerospace applications.

title: Emirates Orders 65 More Boeing 777X Airplanes
summary: Emirates has placed a new order for 65 Boeing 777-9 passenger jets, increasing its total 777X order book to 270 airplanes and solidifying its position as the largest 777X customer. This expansion aims to fuel future growth and meet growing travel demand, reinforcing Emirates' long-term commitment to its partnership with Boeing. The 777-9 aircraft will offer significant efficiency improvements and an enhanced passenger experience.

title: Boeing Distribution (NYSE: BA) debuts ecommerce hub with AI search, single login
summary: Boeing (NYSE: BA) has launched a new unified e-commerce platform for Boeing Distribution, consolidating parts, services, and ordering into a single digital destination at shop.boeing.com/distribution. This platform features an AI-powered smart search, real-time inventory visibility, mobile access, and a single login, aiming to simplify interactions for customers across commercial, business, general aviation, vertical lift, and defense sectors. The launch is part of broader operational upgrades, including enhanced Aircraft on Ground (AOG) access and a new enterprise resource planning system to improve efficiency and customer support.

title: Boeing plans to triple deliveries of Patriot missile seekers - Bloomberg
summary: Boeing Co. plans to triple its deliveries of Patriot missile seekers by building a new 40,000-square-foot production facility to meet high demand for the air defense system. This expansion comes as air and missile defense systems are crucial for many countries, particularly in Europe, amid the threat of aggression from Russia. The company anticipated this demand and is prepared to expand production further if needed.

title: Citizens Financial Group Inc. RI Grows Position in The Boeing Company $BA
summary: Citizens Financial Group Inc. RI significantly increased its stake in The Boeing Company by 94.9% in Q2, acquiring an additional 5,912 shares. Institutional investors collectively own 64.82% of Boeing, despite the company reporting a large EPS miss in its latest quarterly earnings. Analyst consensus remains a "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $240.30, following mixed insider activity including a sale by an EVP and a buy by a director.

title: Cornerstone Advisors LLC Purchases 5,600 Shares of General Dynamics Corporation $GD
summary: Cornerstone Advisors LLC increased its stake in General Dynamics Corporation by 26.3% in the second quarter, acquiring 5,600 additional shares. Insider activity shows net selling recently, with Director Mark Malcolm and VP Christopher J. Brady selling substantial shares. General Dynamics exceeded quarterly EPS and revenue estimates and holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from Wall Street analysts.

title: Ethiopian Airlines Commits to Order 11 Boeing 737 MAX Airplanes
summary: Ethiopian Airlines has committed to purchase 11 additional Boeing 737 MAX jets, an agreement signed at the Dubai Airshow. This expansion will allow Africa's largest carrier to grow its regional and international networks and enhance its Addis Ababa hub. The order reinforces the airline's long-standing partnership with Boeing and its strategic growth plans.

title: Stocks rise at start of week as Nvidia, data await: Markets Wrap
summary: US stock futures, particularly in technology, advanced at the start of the week as investors anticipated Nvidia Corp.'s earnings and the release of delayed economic data. Markets are experiencing a reprieve after recent volatility, with attention also turning to comments from Federal Reserve officials regarding potential interest rate cuts amidst economic uncertainty caused by delayed reports. Corporate news also saw Boeing, Societe Generale, TotalEnergies, and Prosus making significant announcements.

title: Some Things Are Going Right, Says Jim Cramer While Mentioning Boeing (BA)
summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Boeing (NYSE:BA) as one of the stocks where "some things are going right," despite recent dips in share price. He previously advised investors to "stay long" on Boeing, particularly if its cash flow issues were resolved. Cramer noted the resolution of a strike and improvement in the defense sector as positive indicators.

title: Southwest Airlines Co. $LUV Shares Sold by Geode Capital Management LLC
summary: Geode Capital Management LLC reduced its stake in Southwest Airlines Co. by 3.8% in Q2 2025, selling 547,898 shares and ending with 13,961,084 shares valued at approximately $452 million. This comes as Southwest Airlines' Board approved a $2.0 billion share repurchase program, signaling management's belief that the stock may be undervalued. The company reported a ($0.38) EPS on $6.17 billion in revenue for the quarter, with analysts maintaining a "Hold" rating and a consensus target price of $33.73.

title: Ethiopian Airlines Orders 11 More Boeing 737 MAX 8 Aircraft
summary: Ethiopian Airlines has ordered an additional 11 Boeing 737 MAX 8 aircraft, continuing a strong relationship with Boeing despite past challenges. This order was announced at the Dubai Airshow 2025 and will increase the airline's total 737 MAX 8 orders to 39 aircraft. Ethiopian Airlines is also evaluating further purchases of Boeing 787 Dreamliners, highlighting its commitment to Boeing for both narrowbody and widebody fleets.

title: The Boeing Company $BA Shares Sold by Intech Investment Management LLC
summary: Intech Investment Management LLC significantly reduced its stake in The Boeing Company ($BA) by 7.4% in the second quarter, selling 2,776 shares. This article details additional institutional investor activity, insider transactions including share purchases by a director and sales by an EVP, and recent financial performance and analyst ratings for Boeing.

title: Air Senegal Commits to First Boeing 737 MAX Order
summary: Air Senegal has committed to ordering nine Boeing 737 MAX airplanes, marking its largest-ever fleet purchase and first Boeing order since 2004. This acquisition will enable the West African airline to expand its regional and international network, including new routes to Europe, the Middle East, and the Americas. The 737-8 jets offer operational efficiency, reducing fuel use and emissions by 20% and are well-suited to meet Africa's rising air travel demand.

title: Pinnacle Financial Group LLC IL Acquires Shares of 1,373 The Boeing Company $BA
summary: Pinnacle Financial Group LLC IL acquired a new stake of 1,373 shares in The Boeing Company, valued at approximately $288,000 during the second quarter. Other institutional investors like Piscataqua Savings Bank and Monetary Management Group Inc. also adjusted their holdings in Boeing. The article details Boeing's stock performance, recent earnings, analyst ratings, and insider transactions, reflecting a "Moderate Buy" consensus.

title: Rothschild Investment LLC Increases Stock Holdings in The Boeing Company $BA
summary: Rothschild Investment LLC increased its stake in The Boeing Company (NYSE:BA) by 10.9% in the second quarter, bringing its total holdings to 19,679 shares valued at $4.123 million. Other hedge funds also adjusted their positions in Boeing, while the company's stock experienced a slight dip and posted a significant earnings per share miss for the quarter. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for Boeing with a target price of $240.30.

title: #Ethiopian Airlines commits to purchase 11 additional Boeing 737 MAX aircraft Ethiopian Airlines has signed an agreement to purchase 11 new Boeing 737-8 MAX jets, strengthening its position as #Africa’s largest carrier and expanding what is already the co
summary: Ethiopian Airlines has committed to purchasing 11 new Boeing 737-8 MAX aircraft, announced at the Dubai Airshow. This acquisition will bolster its capacity for regional and international routes, aligning with its long-term growth strategy. The deal further strengthens the airline's partnership with Boeing and its position as Africa's largest carrier.

title: Boeing Distribution launches unified ecommerce platform
summary: Boeing [NYSE: BA] announced the launch of a new, unified ecommerce website for Boeing Distribution, marking a key milestone in its modernization and integration efforts. This platform consolidates Boeing Distribution's product and service offerings into a single digital destination, aiming to simplify interactions for customers and suppliers. The initiative is part of broader efforts to enhance operations and customer experience, including improved AOG access and a new ERP system.

title: Guarantor: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC is offering $605,000 in Auto Callable Contingent Interest Notes, fully and unconditionally guaranteed by JPMorgan Chase & Co., linked to the performance of the Dow Jones Industrial Average, Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund, and S&P 500 Equal Weight Index. These notes offer contingent interest payments if underlying values meet specific barriers but carry significant principal loss risk, particularly if not automatically called and underlying values fall below trigger levels. The offering outlines payment mechanics, risk considerations, and tax implications for investors.

title: Understanding Enhanced Flight Vision Systems (EFVS): A Game-Changer for Safer Landings
summary: Enhanced Flight Vision Systems (EFVS) are becoming crucial for modern aviation, especially in challenging weather and busy airspaces. AerSale's AerAware system utilizes advanced technology like infrared cameras and synthetic vision, projected onto a heads-up or wearable display, to significantly improve pilots' situational awareness and safety during all phases of flight. Beyond safety, AerAware offers operational and financial benefits by reducing holding times, expanding runway access, minimizing incident risks, and optimizing aircraft utilization for airlines.

title: Valuation Check: Is Spirit AeroSystems (SPR) Undervalued After Recent Share Price Dip?
summary: Spirit AeroSystems (SPR) has shown a modest dip in share price recently despite steady year-to-date gains and strong revenue growth, prompting valuation questions. Currently trading at a 0.7x price-to-sales ratio, significantly below its sector peers, the stock appears undervalued. However, persistent net losses and sector volatility could impact near-term momentum, though a discounted cash flow (DCF) model suggests a much higher fair value.

title: Bahrain's Gulf Air to add more Boeing 787 Dreamliner planes to its fleet (BA:NYSE)
summary: Bahrain's Gulf Air has finalized a deal with Boeing to purchase at least 12 Boeing 787 Dreamliner planes. This agreement stems from an earlier announcement in July regarding the carrier's intent to buy 12 aircraft with an option for six more. The deal was sealed at the Dubai Airshow.

title: Boeing (NYSE: BA) expands Gulf Air 787 Dreamliner fleet with 15 new jets in firm order
summary: Gulf Air has finalized a firm order for 15 Boeing 787 Dreamliners, with options for 3 more, significantly expanding its long-haul fleet and increasing its total 787 order book to 17 aircraft. This order, placed at the Dubai Airshow, will support the airline's capacity growth, new routes to Asia, Europe, and the U.S., and sustainability goals. Currently operating 10 787s, Gulf Air aims to enhance its international network and passenger experience with these additional fuel-efficient jets.

title: Boeing (NYSE: BA) projects 1,370 widebody jets for Middle East airlines by 2044
summary: Boeing forecasts a significant expansion of the Middle East's airplane fleet, predicting it will more than double by 2044. This growth is driven by increasing passenger traffic, tourism, trade, and hub investment, leading to a demand for 2,950 new aircraft deliveries, including 1,370 widebody jets and 1,430 single-aisle aircraft. The report also projects a need for $455 billion in commercial services and 234,000 new aviation personnel.

title: Boeing rolls out unified ecommerce website to streamline global distribution
summary: Boeing has launched a new unified ecommerce website for its distribution division, aiming to modernize and integrate how customers purchase parts and access services globally. This new platform consolidates Boeing Distribution's entire portfolio, replacing previous separate portals and featuring AI-powered search, real-time inventory visibility, and mobile-friendly access. The initiative is part of a broader, yearlong effort to enhance operational performance, customer support, and supply chain efficiency across Boeing Global Services.

title: Herzfeld Credit Income Fund (NASDAQ: HERZ) sets $11M year-end stock and cash distribution
summary: Herzfeld Credit Income Fund (NASDAQ: HERZ) announced a year-end distribution of approximately $11 million, estimated at $0.6867 per share, payable in stock and cash. The fund corrected previous details, setting the Ex-Date for December 31, 2025, and Payment Date for December 30, 2025. Additionally, HERZ will transition to monthly distributions of net investment income starting January 2026 and will henceforth calculate Net Asset Value (NAV) monthly, anticipating cost savings.

title: Boeing Lands Another Major 787 Win As Gulf Air Confirms 15-Jet Order
summary: Boeing finalized a deal with Gulf Air for 15 Boeing 787 Dreamliners, with options for three additional jets, expanding Gulf Air's total 787 order book to 17. This deal is part of Gulf Air's strategy to increase capacity and strengthen long-haul operations, while Boeing projects significant fleet growth for Middle Eastern airlines by 2044. The article also highlights recent deals with Emirates, Ethiopian Airlines, and Air Senegal.

title: Bedell Frazier Investment Counselling LLC Has $1.73 Million Stock Position in The Boeing Company $BA
summary: Bedell Frazier Investment Counselling LLC has reduced its stake in The Boeing Company (NYSE:BA) by 16.4%, now holding 8,241 shares valued at $1.73 million. Other institutional investors like Vanguard Group Inc., Geode Capital Management LLC, and Charles Schwab Investment Management Inc. have increased their holdings. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for Boeing, with a consensus price target of $240.30, despite the company missing its Q3 earnings per share estimates.

title: Gulf Air Increases Boeing 787 Dreamliner Order
summary: Gulf Air has finalized a firm order for 15 Boeing 787 Dreamliners, with options for three more, expanding on an earlier commitment from July 2025. This agreement brings Gulf Air's total order book for the 787 Dreamliner to 17, reinforcing its strategy for increased capacity and long-haul operations. The order underscores the strong relationship between Gulf Air and Boeing, and highlights the 787's role in the airline's network expansion across Asia, Europe, and the U.S.

title: Middle East Airlines Enter New Era of Growth as Region's Fleet Will More Than Double by 2044
summary: Boeing projects that the Middle East's airline fleet will more than double by 2044, driven by increasing passenger traffic, tourism, trade, and hub development. The region is expected to require nearly 1,400 new widebody passenger jets and 185 freighters, with strong growth also anticipated in the single-aisle and low-cost carrier segments. This expansion will necessitate $455 billion in commercial aviation services and 234,000 new aviation personnel.

title: Ethiopian Airlines selects RTX's Collins Aerospace for premium seating solutions across multiple fleets
summary: Ethiopian Airlines has partnered with RTX's Collins Aerospace to enhance its premium in-flight experience across multiple aircraft fleets. The agreement, signed at the Dubai Air Show, will see Collins' Elevation suites installed in 11 new Airbus A350-900 aircraft and Parallel Diamond business class seats in 56 Boeing 737 MAX aircraft. This collaboration aims to provide luxury and consistent comfort for passengers, reflecting Ethiopian Airlines' commitment to world-class service and innovation.

title: Last month, Southwest Airlines unveiled a new cabin design that’s already rolling out in new planes across its fleet. It includes slimmer seats, updated amenities, and lots and lots of blue. The new cabin was revealed on October 14, and there are currently six a
summary: Southwest Airlines has unveiled a new cabin design featuring slimmer seats, updated amenities, and a predominantly blue interior, which is already being rolled out on new aircraft. This change is part of a larger "transformational plan" by Southwest, which has included altering iconic brand features like its "bags fly free" policy to compete with airlines that charge ancillary premium fees. Customer reactions to the changes range from disappointment over seat comfort to suggestions for improved customer service and the introduction of a first-class seating area.

title: Saab, Boeing and BAE Systems to collaborate on Next-Gen pilot training
summary: Saab, Boeing, and BAE Systems have signed a Letter of Intent to collaborate on the Royal Air Force fast-jet trainer programme, using the T-7 as the core training system. This collaboration aims to provide innovative training solutions for RAF pilots, supporting future international pilot training opportunities and potentially creating UK jobs through local assembly. The T-7A Red Hawk, selected by the U.S. Air Force, will be central to this advanced live, virtual, and constructive training system.

title: FlyDubai orders 150 Airbus A321neo aircraft, expanding its fleet beyond Boeing for the first time
summary: FlyDubai has placed an order for 150 Airbus A321neo aircraft, an estimated $24 billion purchase, marking the first time the carrier expands its fleet beyond Boeing. This deal, announced at the Dubai Air Show, will more than double FlyDubai's current fleet and is part of Dubai's broader plan to expand its aviation infrastructure, including a new five-runway airport. The order follows other significant purchases by regional airlines, including an earlier Boeing order by FlyDubai and recent Airbus orders by Etihad and Boeing 777-9 orders by Emirates.

title: Is LMT Expanding Helicopter Capabilities With Autonomous Upgrades?
summary: Lockheed Martin (LMT) is enhancing its helicopter capabilities through its Sikorsky division, focusing on autonomous upgrades for its Black Hawk family. The integration of MATRIX technology allows for optionally piloted and fully autonomous missions, demonstrated through successful logistics and medical-evacuation operations. These advancements extend beyond defense to civil operations like firefighting, highlighting LMT's commitment to modernizing rotorcraft for various global demands.

title: How Many 777X's Has Boeing Already Started Building?
summary: Boeing has already built four test 777X aircraft and 22 customer-version 777X jets which are currently awaiting delivery. An additional six more 777Xs are in various stages of assembly at the Everett factory. The 777X program has faced multiple delays and is still in the test phase, seeking official Federal Aviation Administration certification, with the first delivery now expected in late 2027.

title: Catalyst Bancorp Announces Sixth Share Repurchase Program
summary: Catalyst Bancorp announced its sixth share repurchase program, authorizing the buyback of up to 205,000 shares, approximately 5% of its outstanding common stock. This strategic move aims to manage capital structure and potentially enhance market presence and shareholder value. Despite strong technical indicators, TipRanks' AI Analyst Spark views CLST as Neutral due to financial challenges like declining revenues and a high P/E ratio.

title: Will L3Harris Technologies (LHX) Automation Drive Sustainable Efficiency Gains in Missile Manufacturing?
summary: L3Harris Technologies is expanding its solid rocket motor production capacity and strengthening international partnerships in defense technologies, including AI and autonomous systems. Despite ongoing supply-chain issues, several companies in the sector are poised for further benefits as production normalizes in 2026. This article identifies seven specific stocks that are expected to perform well in the aerospace recovery through 2026.

title: Evergy Inc. (NASDAQ:EVRG) Receives Average Recommendation of "Buy" from Brokerages
summary: Evergy Inc. (NASDAQ:EVRG) has received a consensus "Buy" recommendation from eleven analysts, with an average one-year price target of $80.72. This comes despite the company missing quarterly earnings and revenue expectations, although full-year EPS is projected at $3.83. Evergy also increased its quarterly dividend to $0.695, equating to an annualized yield near 3.8%.

title: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (KTOS): Valuation Check After New Vancouver PT6 Engine Overhaul Facility Expansion
summary: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (KTOS) recently expanded its North American service network by opening a new PT6 engine overhaul facility in Vancouver. Despite a significant 191% year-to-date share price return, an analysis suggests KTOS is currently 23.5% undervalued, with a fair value estimate of $100.56 against a last close of $76.91, driven by its leadership in tactical drones and missile propulsion. However, some concern exists regarding its high price-to-sales ratio, trading at 10.1x revenue compared to the industry average of 3.1x, indicating potential valuation risk if market sentiment shifts.

title: FAS Wealth Partners Inc. Purchases 10,525 Shares of The Boeing Company $BA
summary: FAS Wealth Partners Inc. significantly increased its stake in The Boeing Company by purchasing an additional 10,525 shares, bringing its total holdings to 13,691 shares valued at $2,869,000. Other hedge funds also adjusted their positions in Boeing, which currently has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating and an average target price of $233.17 from analysts. The article also details recent insider transactions and the company's financial performance.

title: RTX (RTX): Valuation Check After Raytheon’s New Cloud and AI Partnership With AWS
summary: RTX (RTX) has partnered with Amazon Web Services to integrate cloud-native and AI-driven models into satellite data processing and mission control, bolstering its defense technology portfolio. While the stock has seen significant gains, trading near record highs, Simply Wall St's narrative suggests it is 9.8% undervalued with a fair value of $193.79, driven by strategic portfolio optimization and expected margin improvements. However, a traditional earnings multiple view shows less upside.

title: Airbus 3D printed over 25,000 parts in 2025 with Stratasys technology
summary: Airbus is producing over 25,000 flight-ready 3D-printed parts annually using Stratasys technology, significantly transforming aircraft manufacturing and maintenance. This collaboration has led to a 43% weight reduction and an 85% lead time reduction for parts in the Airbus A350, contributing to cost savings and faster production. The integration of certified additive manufacturing is seen as a crucial step towards sustainable aviation and a mainstream production method in the aerospace industry.

title: Inside Boeing's Acquisition of Spirit AeroSystems
summary: Boeing has finalized its acquisition of Spirit AeroSystems, integrating its Northern Ireland and Scotland operations into Boeing’s UK network. The Northern Ireland facilities will now operate as 'Short Brothers, a Boeing Company', strengthening Boeing's manufacturing and engineering presence in the UK. This strategic move adds around 2,400 employees and significant aerospace manufacturing expertise to Boeing's operations.

title: Boeing unveiling signage after acquisition of Spirit AeroSystems
summary: Boeing is unveiling new signage in Wichita today, marking its return to the community where the aviation company began decades ago, following its acquisition of Spirit AeroSystems. Approximately 15,000 workers across five sites, including Wichita, Dallas, Oklahoma, and Prestwick, Scotland, will integrate into Boeing, with Belfast operations becoming an independent subsidiary, Short Brothers. This acquisition includes all of Spirit's Boeing-related commercial operations, such as fuselages for the 737 program and major structures for other Boeing aircraft.

title: Glenview Trust co Has $44.48 Million Stock Position in The Boeing Company $BA
summary: Glenview Trust Co. has increased its stake in The Boeing Company (NYSE:BA) by 14.2% in Q2, acquiring an additional 26,367 shares to hold a total of 212,274 shares worth $44.48 million. This position represents approximately 0.8% of the firm's portfolio. Company insiders have shown mixed activity, with EVP Uma Amuluru selling some shares while SVP Dana Deasy purchased shares.

title: Boeing-backed Start-up Evio Launches Hybrid-electric Regional Airliner
summary: Evio, a start-up supported by Boeing and Pratt & Whitney Canada, has launched its hybrid-electric regional airliner program. The company released a digital rendering of its planned 76-seat Evio 810 aircraft, aiming to introduce a more sustainable option for regional air travel.

title: Is Leadership Turnover, Ajax Risk And Steady Dividend Altering The Investment Case For General Dynamics (GD)?
summary: General Dynamics recently promoted a new president, announced a controller transition, and declared a $1.50 quarterly dividend, while also facing potential cancellation of the U.K.’s £6.30 billion Ajax armored vehicle program due to safety concerns. These changes, combined with ongoing dividend payments and program-specific risks, highlight how the company balances governance stability with exposure to significant defense revenues. The article explores how the Ajax program uncertainty and leadership changes could influence General Dynamics' investment outlook.

title: Neil Thwaites promoted to ‘Vice President of Global Sales & California Commercial Performance’ for Alaska Airlines and Hawaiian Airlines
summary: Neil Thwaites has been promoted to "Vice President of Global Sales & California Commercial Performance" for Alaska Airlines and Hawaiian Airlines. This newly created role emphasizes a strategic vision to connect guests globally, particularly as the airlines expand their international network. Thwaites will lead sales activities for both airlines, focusing on indirect revenue growth and expanding the Atmos for Business program, building on his previous success as Regional Vice President of California for Alaska Airlines.

title: Analysts Offer Insights on Industrial Goods Companies: Aaon (AAON) and Boeing (BA)
summary: Analysts have provided bullish sentiments on Industrial Goods companies Aaon (AAON) and Boeing (BA). Ryan Merkel from William Blair maintained a Buy rating on Aaon, while Gautam Khanna from TD Cowen and Bernstein reiterated Buy ratings on Boeing, highlighting significant upside potential for the latter.

title: RBC Bearings stock reaches all-time high of 449.8 USD
summary: RBC Bearings stock has achieved an all-time high of 449.8 USD, reflecting a 33.14% increase over the past year. This surge is supported by strong fiscal Q2 2025 results, which surpassed earnings and revenue expectations, and positive analyst upgrades from Truist Securities and BofA Securities due to growth in aerospace and defense markets.

title: Boeing's return to Wichita: Company signage added to campus
summary: Boeing is making a symbolic return to Wichita with the addition of company signage to its campus. This development signifies a physical representation of Boeing's renewed presence in the area. The article highlights this visual change as an update to the company's activities in Wichita.

title: How a Greenville turbine factory earned a White House shout out
summary: A Greenville factory operated by GE Vernova, a General Electric spinoff, received a White House shout out for its role in a $14.2 billion deal to ship gas-fired turbines to Saudi Arabia. This deal was part of a larger $1 trillion investment commitment from Saudi Arabia touted by the Trump administration. While the recognition highlighted GE Vernova's significant contributions to energy demand and job creation, the impressive figures did not represent new promises for South Carolina but rather reaffirmed existing investments, including a substantial $160 million for the Greenville facility and plans to hire 650 new workers.

title: Ramon Sanchez Joins Firefly Aerospace as Chief Operating Officer
summary: Firefly Aerospace has appointed Ramon Sanchez as its new Chief Operating Officer, effective December 22, 2025. In this role, Sanchez will oversee daily operations, focusing on enhancing safety, quality, and reliability in the production of launch vehicles, lunar landers, and spacecraft. His extensive background from Boeing, including experience with heavy launch vehicles and satellite constellations, is expected to drive Firefly's production scaling and operational excellence.

title: Water board proposes $600K Boeing fine
summary: The Los Angeles Regional Water Quality Control Board has proposed a $600,000 fine against The Boeing Company for nearly 40 stormwater discharge violations at the Santa Susana Field Laboratory. These violations, occurring between 2023 and 2025, involved hazardous chemicals and metals impacting watersheds in Los Angeles and Ventura counties. Environmental groups are simultaneously challenging the state's cleanup agreement for the site, arguing it allows most contamination to remain.

title: Has GE’s 2025 Surge Left Limited Upside for New Investors?
summary: General Electric (GE) has experienced significant gains over the past three years, driven by its business restructuring into more focused aerospace and energy companies. Despite this surge, a valuation analysis indicates GE is currently overvalued by approximately 29.0% based on a Discounted Cash Flow model and marginally expensive by its Price-to-Earnings ratio. This suggests that much of the future upside might already be priced into the stock, offering limited margin of safety for new investors.

title: American becomes first US airline to debut long-range Airbus A321XLR
summary: American Airlines is set to become the first U.S. airline to debut the long-range Airbus A321XLR, featuring its new Flagship Suites and a premium-heavy configuration. The aircraft, which seats 155 passengers, will initially operate on premium transcontinental routes like New York to Los Angeles, before expanding to new international destinations in Europe and South America. This move aims to enhance American's premium offerings and compete more effectively with rivals like United and Delta.

title: Sustainability
summary: Boeing is committed to advancing sustainability in aerospace through efficient aircraft design, global collaboration on Sustainable Aviation Fuels (SAF), and sustainable internal operations. The company's aircraft reduce fuel use, CO2 emissions, and noise, while their ecoDemonstrator program tests new technologies. Boeing also focuses on restoring industrial sites and transparent reporting of its sustainability efforts.

title: Is United Airlines Stock Underperforming the Nasdaq?
summary: United Airlines Holdings, Inc. (UAL) is a large-cap U.S. carrier with a market cap of $34.9 billion, focusing on fleet modernization and premium services. While UAL stock has fallen 6.6% from its 52-week high and underperformed the Nasdaq Composite over the past three months, Wall Street analysts maintain a "Strong Buy" rating with a potential upside of 16.1%. The company is benefiting from renewed analyst confidence and strategic partnerships to modernize sales technology.

title: How a Greenville turbine factory earned a White House shoutout
summary: A Greenville turbine factory, GE Vernova, was recognized by the White House for its role in a $14.2 billion deal to ship eight gas-fired turbines to Saudi Arabia, part of a larger $1 trillion Saudi investment commitment in the U.S. The company plans to significantly increase its output of natural gas turbines and hire 650 new workers at its Greenville plant, investing at least $160 million there. This expansion continues GE's nearly six-decade presence and investment in South Carolina.

title: Bayesian Capital Management LP Sells 18,500 Shares of The Boeing Company $BA
summary: Bayesian Capital Management LP significantly reduced its stake in The Boeing Company, selling 18,500 shares in Q2, leaving it with 18,400 shares valued at approximately $3.855 million. This divestment occurred despite Boeing reporting a revenue beat in its latest earnings, though it missed EPS expectations. Insider transactions show varied activity, while analysts largely maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a $233.17 price target.

title: RTX : Collins Elbit Vision Systems' Zero-G Helmet Mounted Display System+ achieves key milestone with U.S. Navy
summary: Collins Elbit Vision Systems (CEVS), a joint venture involving RTX's Collins Aerospace and Elbit Systems of America, has completed the Critical Design Review for its Zero-G Helmet Mounted Display System+ (HMDS+). This advanced sixth-generation system is designed for the U.S. Navy's F/A-18E/F Super Hornets and EA-18G Growlers, aiming to enhance battlespace awareness, reduce aircrew strain, and improve safety. Initial operational capability is projected for 2027, with plans for fielding on over 750 U.S. Navy and Royal Australian Air Force aircraft.

title: Firefly Aerospace Appoints Boeing Veteran Ramon Sanchez as Chief Operating Officer
summary: Firefly Aerospace has named Ramon Sanchez, a former Boeing senior director and Army veteran, as its Chief Operating Officer. Sanchez will oversee the company's daily operations and the production of launch vehicles, lunar landers, and spacecraft. His extensive experience in improving complex space production lines and building cross-functional teams is expected to be a valuable asset for Firefly Aerospace.

title: The Boeing Company $BA Shares Bought by Mark Asset Management LP
summary: Mark Asset Management LP increased its stake in The Boeing Company (NYSE:BA) by 6.5% during the second quarter, making it their 13th largest position. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their holdings in Boeing. The article also details recent analyst ratings and insider trading activities for BA stock.

title: Boeing, SkyGrid and Wisk introduce concept of operations for automated flight rules
summary: Boeing, SkyGrid, and Wisk have introduced a Concept of Operations for Automated Flight Rules (AFR), a framework designed to integrate highly automated and uncrewed aircraft into airspace safely and efficiently. This new operating mode complements existing Visual Flight Rules (VFR) and Instrument Flight Rules (IFR) by leveraging advanced automation to manage air traffic and separate aircraft in specific scenarios, aiming to reduce air traffic controller workload and enhance trajectory flexibility. The AFR Framework provides use cases and system implications and serves as a roadmap for future rulemaking and research within the aviation ecosystem.

title: Firefly Aerospace Stock Surges, But I Still See A Speculative Buy (NASDAQ:FLY)
summary: Firefly Aerospace (FLY) remains an attractive speculative buy despite a recent 27% stock surge, with a reduced price target of $37.38 for 2026, offering approximately 60% upside. Recent positive developments include its inclusion in the Russell 2000/3000 indices, a partnership with Volta Space, and the appointment of a new COO. Key risks still include potential mission failures, development setbacks, and intense competition in the emerging space sector.

title: Billings Flying Service Acquired by Quanta Aviation Services
summary: Billings Flying Service (BFS) has been acquired by Quanta Services, Inc. (NYSE: PWR), aiming to expand BFS's offerings into the utility and construction market and enhance Quanta Aviation Services' fleet with CH-47D aircraft. BFS will maintain its existing contracts and operational structure, while integrating into the Quanta Aviation Services family. This acquisition is expected to provide BFS with greater resources and long-term stability for growth.

title: Military Rotorcraft
summary: Boeing's Military Rotorcraft division provides reliable rotorcraft for attack, transport, humanitarian, and rescue missions in austere conditions, serving the United States Army, Navy, Marines, Air Force, and allied defense forces in over 20 countries. Boeing focuses on continuous innovation to meet current and future challenges, including developing new rotorcraft concepts and improving capabilities for manned and unmanned teaming. Their product lineup includes the AH-6 Little Bird, AH-64 Apache, H-47 Chinook, MH-139 Grey Wolf, and V-22 Osprey.

title: ATI Rides Aerospace Demand And Strong Cash Flow To New Highs
summary: ATI Inc. (NYSE: ATI) has seen significant success due to booming aerospace and defense demand, along with strong cash flow and share buybacks. The specialty metals maker, headquartered in Dallas, Texas, leverages its vertical integration, alloy technology, and long-term contracts with major aerospace players to maintain a competitive edge. While the company faces risks like cyclical end-market exposure and raw material price volatility, its strong return on invested capital and attractive valuation against peers suggest potential for future growth.

title: SkyWest's EPS Estimates Northbound: Should Investors Buy the Stock?
summary: SkyWest, Inc. (SKYW) is experiencing upward revisions in its EPS estimates for late 2025 and 2026, driven by increased flying contract rates, fleet modernization, and shareholder-friendly initiatives. The company's strong balance sheet, attractive valuation, and a #2 (Buy) Zacks Rank suggest it is a compelling investment opportunity despite macroeconomic uncertainties and pilot shortages.

title: US FAA to review Boeing cockpit alerting system for MAX 10
summary: The U.S. Federal Aviation Administration announced it will review Boeing's proposed enhanced flight crew alerting system for the 737 MAX 10, which includes a synthetic enhanced angle-of-attack system. This review is part of a Congressional requirement for safety enhancements within three years of MAX 10 certification, following two fatal 737 MAX crashes. The FAA will also monitor Boeing to ensure timely service information for airlines to incorporate these safety improvements across all 737 MAX models.

title: Boeing’s Baa3 rating affirmed by Moody’s, outlook now stable
summary: Moody's Ratings has affirmed Boeing's Baa3 senior unsecured rating and P-3 commercial paper rating, changing the outlook from negative to stable following Boeing's acquisition of Spirit AeroSystems. The rating agency also upgraded Spirit's senior unsecured and secured notes to Baa3 with a stable outlook. This reflects expected improvements in Boeing's quality control and production rate increases, supported by strong financial flexibility and a robust order backlog.

title: Cleveland-Cliffs Stock (NYSE: CLF) Slides After S&P Downgrade: Latest News, Forecasts, and Analyst Views for December 12, 2025
summary: Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF) shares dropped after S&P Global Ratings downgraded its credit rating to B+ due to elevated leverage and a stressed credit profile. The downgrade highlights concerns about refinancing costs and financial flexibility, even as S&P forecasts a significant earnings rebound for 2026. Investors are now closely watching deleveraging progress, steel pricing, and the outcome of the POSCO partnership.

title: C-17 Globemaster
summary: The C-17 Globemaster III is a versatile, high-wing, four-engine transport aircraft used for delivering large equipment, supplies, and troops to austere airfields globally. It combines long-range capability with short-field performance and oversized payload handling, supporting tactical airlift, strategic delivery, and humanitarian relief missions. Boeing provides comprehensive sustainment, training, and support to maintain the C-17 fleets' mission capability for customers worldwide.

title: Water board proposes a $600K fine against Boeing Co.
summary: The Los Angeles Regional Water Quality Control Board has proposed a $600,000 fine against Boeing Co. for nearly 40 stormwater discharge violations at the Santa Susana Field Laboratory. This proposed fine comes as environmental groups are simultaneously pursuing a lawsuit challenging the state’s cleanup agreement for the contaminated site. Boeing maintains high compliance with discharge permits but faces pressure from advocates demanding a full cleanup.

title: 787 Dreamliner
summary: The Boeing 787 Dreamliner family is designed for efficiency and versatility, enabling airlines to establish new nonstop routes and expand their networks while offering exceptional passenger comfort. The article provides technical specifications for the 787-8, 787-9, and 787-10 models, detailing passenger capacity, range, length, wingspan, height, and engine types. Since its inception, the 787 Dreamliner has flown over a billion passengers, opened 523 new nonstop routes, and reached more than 520 destinations across 85+ countries.

title: Law professor sues Boeing, alleging he breathed in toxic fumes on a Delta flight
summary: A law professor is suing Boeing for $40 million, alleging he suffered severe health issues after inhaling toxic fumes during a Delta flight in August 2024. The lawsuit claims that contaminated cabin air, which smelled like "dirty socks," caused symptoms including dizziness, nausea, and memory loss, as well as mental anguish and lost wages. Boeing has not commented on the suit, while Delta declined to comment but is replacing auxiliary power units on some Airbus aircraft to address similar fume incidents.

title: Mayer Brown advises Boeing on strategic transactions in connection with Spirit AeroSystems acquisition
summary: Mayer Brown advised The Boeing Company on a series of M&A transactions related to Boeing's acquisition of Spirit AeroSystems. This included advising on Spirit's divestitures to Airbus SE, Tex Tech Industries, Inc. (Fiber Materials Inc.), and Composites Technology Research Malaysia Sdn. Bhd. (Spirit AeroSystems Malaysia Sdn. Bhd.). The team was led by M&A partners Jason Quintana and Magnus Karlberg.

title: U.S. shares lower at close of trade; Dow Jones Industrial Average down 0.51%
summary: U.S. equities closed lower on Friday, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average falling 0.51%, the S&P 500 declining 1.07%, and the NASDAQ Composite dropping 1.69%. Technology, Industrials, and Oil & Gas sectors were among the biggest movers, while various stocks experienced significant gains and losses. Commodity prices saw mixed movements, with gold futures rising, crude oil and Brent oil falling, and currency pairs EUR/USD and USD/JPY showing slight changes.

summary: Clearfield Capital Management LP recently purchased 108,945 shares of Alaska Air Group, Inc. (NYSE:ALK) valued at approximately $5.39 million, making it their 11th largest holding. title: XCF Global says U.S. policy push is speeding up adoption of sustainable aviation fuel
summary: XCF Global, Inc. states that U.S. policy support, including the SAF Grand Challenge and state-level low-carbon fuel standards, is significantly accelerating the adoption of sustainable aviation fuel. The company projects the U.S. SAF market to grow from $860 million in 2024 to nearly $7 billion by 2030, driven by incentives for wider SAF adoption and ambitions to produce 35 billion gallons annually by 2050. XCF Global is expanding its production capacity with a $300 million investment in a second facility, aiming to double output and contribute to the "Make More in America Initiative" while positioning the U.S. as a leader in sustainable aviation.

title: Expanding? How GE Is Addressing Worldwide Jet Engine Demand
summary: GE Aerospace, in collaboration with CFM International, faces intensified pressure due to accelerated narrowbody fleet expansion and rising aircraft deliveries. To meet this unprecedented demand, GE is significantly expanding its manufacturing footprint, particularly for the LEAP engine family, and investing heavily in MRO capabilities, supply-chain strengthening, and R&D for future propulsion technologies. The company's strategy aims to address current volumes, prepare for future needs, and ensure reliable, cost-efficient engine delivery and maintenance worldwide.

title: Boeing Stock (BA) After the Bell on Dec. 12, 2025: Key Headlines, Analyst Forecasts, and What to Watch Before the Next Market Open
summary: Boeing stock (NYSE: BA) finished higher on Friday, December 12, 2025, despite a broader market decline, closing up 1.83% at $204.38. Key drivers for this performance included progress on 737 MAX certification, ongoing discussions around the Air Force One replacement, and a new "Buy" rating from Citi with a $265 price target. title: Is American Airlines Done As A Big Three Airline As United CEO Claims?

While American Airlines (AAL) faces challenges—including stock underperformance, debt, and customer satisfaction issues—it currently holds the largest U.S. airline market share and is implementing premium service upgrades and loyalty program initiatives to counter these perceptions and improve its standing.

title: 737 MAX
summary: The Boeing 737 MAX family offers exceptional capability, flexibility, and value, with technological advancements resulting in a 20% reduction in CO2 emissions and fuel use. Its commonality across models (737-7, 737-8, 737-9, 737-10) provides airlines with significant efficiency savings in operations, maintenance, and training.

summary: Boeing emphasizes a strong commitment to ethical behavior and compliance across its global operations, guided by values of safety, quality, integrity, and transparency. The company's comprehensive program includes an annual Code of Conduct reaffirmation, employee training, and proactive avenues for raising concerns without fear of retaliation. Overseen by a Chief Compliance Officer and a Compliance Risk Management Board, Boeing rigorously implements anti-corruption measures and internal controls to ensure adherence to global laws and regulations.

summary: The F-15EX Eagle is highlighted as an unmatched fighter designed to anchor tactical air forces with its superior payload, range, and speed. It features digital fly-by-wire controls, an all-glass cockpit, modern mission systems, and the capacity to carry hypersonic weapons. The article emphasizes its tactical, operational, and economical advantages, including high weapons capacity, advanced electronic warfare systems, rapid fielding capabilities, and lower acquisition and life cycle costs compared to new fifth-generation fighters.

title: Air Force One
summary: This article details the history and features of the Boeing aircraft designated as Air Force One, emphasizing Boeing's long-standing role in providing transport for U.S. presidents since 1943. summary: The MH-139 Grey Wolf is a multimission utility helicopter produced in the United States, incorporating state-of-the-art technology for safe, quick, and efficient operation across various missions. Its capabilities include patrol, firefighting, search and rescue, VIP, troop, and cargo transport, with a strong emphasis on safety features and efficient sustainment and training. The U.S. Air Force utilizes the MH-139 for intercontinental ballistic missile monitoring and transport of government officials.

title: Boeing Vs Airbus: Which Planes Are Safer According To Accident Stats?
summary: Despite recent incidents involving Boeing aircraft, an analysis of U.S. accident data from 2014-2024 reveals that Boeing and Airbus have very similar safety records for issues related to equipment malfunction. While Boeing operates more flights and therefore has more accidents in sheer numbers, when adjusted for flight volume, both manufacturers exhibit comparable safety. Misinformation and increased media scrutiny contribute to Boeing's perceived worse reputation, but ultimately, both aircraft types are equally safe for travelers.

title: Explained: How GE Became a Jet Engine Manufacturer
summary: General Electric transitioned from an electrical and industrial company into a dominant jet engine manufacturer, leveraging its expertise in steam turbines and high-temperature metallurgy. The airline also announced an order for 30 Boeing 787 Dreamliner jets, diversifying its fleet and replacing older, less efficient aircraft. This optimistic outlook comes despite previous setbacks like government shutdowns and tariffs.

title: Boeing beats Airbus in jet orders for first time since 2018 (BA:NYSE)

This recovery was spurred by a strong fourth quarter, regulatory approval to raise production, and improvements in quality control, with a major commitment from Alaska Airlines highlighting renewed confidence. While still facing competition from Airbus, Boeing's narrowbody program achieved its most stable point since 2018, reducing delivery times and improving cash flow.

title: Delta leans on premium flyers for 2026 growth, orders Boeing 787s​​

Delta also placed an order for 30 Boeing 787 widebody jets, with options for 30 more, to diversify its long-haul fleet and strengthen its international routes, particularly to the Atlantic and South America.

title: Boeing turnaround takes hold with highest annual jet deliveries since 2018
summary: Boeing achieved its highest annual jet deliveries since 2018 and surpassed Airbus in net orders for the first time in seven years, indicating a significant turnaround after a series of crises. Analysts believe Boeing's strong order book signals confidence from airlines and predict continued improvement in 2026.

title: Boeing reports most annual aircraft deliveries since 2018
