The change marks a shift for Southwest towards industry norms, allowing for monetization through assigned seating and extra-legroom options, following significant shareholder investment.

title: Embraer/Adani – Reaction and Reality
summary: Embraer and Adani Defence & Aerospace have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to develop an integrated regional transport aircraft ecosystem in India, aiming to collaborate on manufacturing, supply chain, aftermarket services, and pilot training. While the announcement has generated significant positive reactions, particularly regarding the potential for a Final Assembly Line (FAL) in India, critical details like location, investment, and operation timelines remain elusive. The article emphasizes the importance of the word "evaluate" in the MoU, suggesting that the onus is on India to transition from evaluation to construction, given Embraer's cautious approach despite its capabilities.

title: Hanwha Ocean and Algoma Steel sign MOU to support Canadian steel and jobs
summary: Hanwha Ocean and Algoma Steel Inc. have signed a binding Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) for a long-term strategic arrangement. Hanwha Ocean will contribute up to USD $250 million (CAD $345 million) to support Algoma Steel's diversification strategy, including the potential development of a structural steel beam mill in Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario. This partnership aims to strengthen domestic supply chains for Canadian defense procurement, specifically for Hanwha Ocean’s Canadian Patrol Submarine Project (CPSP)-related submarine construction and maintenance and repair (MRO) infrastructure, creating new skilled jobs in Canada.

title: Boeing Posts Q4 Profit on Asset Sale, Higher Deliveries
summary: Boeing reported strong fourth-quarter 2025 results, with revenue of $23.9 billion and net earnings of $8.2 billion, significantly boosted by a $9.6 billion gain from the sale of its Jeppesen ForeFlight unit. The company also achieved its highest annual commercial delivery total since 2018, with 600 aircraft, and a record total backlog of $682 billion, indicating substantial recovery and setting a foundation for future growth. CEO Kelly Ortberg emphasized the company's focus on stable operations, completing development programs, and rebuilding trust after strategically acquiring Spirit AeroSystems.

title: Strs Ohio Decreases Holdings in Alaska Air Group, Inc. $ALK
summary: Strs Ohio significantly reduced its stake in Alaska Air Group, Inc. by 37.8% in the third quarter, now holding 75,557 shares valued at $3,761,000. Despite this, other institutional investors made minor adjustments to their positions, mostly increasing holdings. Alaska Air Group currently holds a "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts with a consensus target price of $71.80, and recently outperformed earnings per share estimates for its latest quarter.

title: Surf Air Mobility Partners with the Hawaii Department of Transportation and BETA Technologies to Advance Electric Aviation in Hawaii with eIPP Application
summary: Surf Air Mobility has partnered with the Hawaii Department of Transportation and BETA Technologies to advance electric aviation in Hawaii. The collaboration involves an eIPP application, aiming to bring electric aircraft solutions to the region. This initiative highlights efforts towards sustainable transportation in the state.

title: GE Aerospace Delta Engine Deal Highlights Growing Long Term Cash Flows
summary: GE Aerospace, a division of General Electric, has secured a significant deal with Delta Air Lines to supply GEnx engines for 30 Boeing 787-10 aircraft, with options for 30 more and long-term service agreements. This contract reinforces GE Aerospace's strategic focus on aviation and is expected to contribute to growing long-term cash flows through both initial equipment sales and recurring aftermarket services. The agreement, valued at a share price of $295.06, highlights the company's strong market position and recent growth in earnings and revenue.

title: FTSE 100 Live: London stocks climb with HSBC taking crown as largest company
summary: London's FTSE 100 climbed, driven by HSBC Holdings PLC taking over AstraZeneca as the largest company in the index, largely due to positive industrial profit data from China. Meanwhile, Dr Martens saw a sales slowdown, and M&G PLC stated it could absorb a £230 million hit from UK ground rent reforms. Gold prices are also seeing upward momentum, with Deutsche Bank suggesting a potential rise to $6,000 an ounce.

title: U.S. Navy Selects New Helmet for Fixed Wing Crews
summary: The U.S. Navy has chosen the Gentex Pursuit helmet as its Next Generation Fixed Wing Helmet (NGFWH) for flight crews of various aircraft including the E-2D, F/A-18, and T-6. This new helmet replaces the older HGU-68P, also made by Gentex, and aims to improve pilot safety, comfort, and mission performance by addressing issues like neck strain and ejection risks. Gentex has been awarded a five-year, $22 million contract to deliver over 5,000 systems.

title: Boeing is set to report earnings before the bell. Here's what Wall Street expects
summary: Boeing is expected to report significantly reduced losses in the fourth quarter of 2025 and a potential return to annual profitability for the first time since 2018. The company delivered 600 airplanes last year and outsold Airbus in net orders, signifying a crucial ramp-up after years of financial challenges. Investors will be keenly watching for updates on delivery pace, certification timelines for various Max models and the 777X, and the performance of its defense business.

title: RTX Corp annual profit forecast in line with expectations at midpoint
summary: RTX Corp announced an annual profit forecast for its 2026 fiscal year that aligns with market expectations, projecting adjusted earnings per share between $6.60 and $6.80. The company anticipates strong performance driven by solid demand for its defense systems and aerospace services, with significant sales increases reported across its Raytheon, Collins Aerospace, and Pratt & Whitney divisions in the fourth quarter. CEO Chris Calio expressed confidence in meeting 2026 financial targets, citing current momentum and plans for expanding production capacity.

title: American Airlines reports fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results
summary: American Airlines (NASDAQ: AAL) reported record fourth-quarter revenue of $14.0 billion and full-year 2025 revenue of $54.6 billion, despite a $325 million negative impact from a government shutdown. The company forecasts full-year 2026 adjusted EPS between $1.70 and $2.70 and expects over $2 billion in free cash flow, driven by strategic investments in customer experience, network, fleet, and loyalty programs. American also announced plans to expand its DFW hub and retrofit older aircraft to enhance premium seating capacity.

title: Boeing Cash, Sales Rise After Most Jet Deliveries Since 2018
summary: Boeing (BA) reported a strong financial quarter, generating cash for the second consecutive time and experiencing a 57% increase in sales. This performance was driven by the highest number of jet deliveries since 2018 and includes a significant gain from the sale of its Jeppesen subsidiary. The results indicate a continued recovery for the US planemaker amid surging orders.

title: Boeing Reports Fourth Quarter Results
summary: Boeing has announced strong fourth-quarter results for 2025, showing significant financial improvement with revenues reaching $23.9 billion, a 57% increase from the previous year. The company reported a substantial gain in EPS, reached record backlog of $682 billion, and increased commercial airplane deliveries, driven by strategic acquisitions and production rate increases. Management expressed confidence in continued recovery and growth, focusing on stable operations and restoring trust.

title: Boeing: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
summary: Boeing Co. (BA) reported a fourth-quarter net income of $8.22 billion, a significant turnaround from a loss in the previous year. Despite a profit of $10.23 per share, adjusted losses of $1.91 per share did not meet Wall Street expectations. The company, however, exceeded revenue forecasts, posting $23.95 billion for the period.

title: Boeing reports total company backlog of $682B, including over 6,100 airplanes
summary: Boeing (BA) announced a total company backlog of $682 billion, which includes orders for over 6,100 airplanes. This news comes as the company prepares to report its Q4 earnings today, January 27, 2026, with options traders anticipating a 4.72% price movement in the aerospace stock. The report highlights substantial demand for Boeing's products and services.

title: Alaska Air Group, Inc. (NYSE:ALK) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: Alaska Air Group (NYSE: ALK) reported Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings, with adjusted net income of $50 million and $293 million respectively. Despite macroeconomic challenges, the company delivered on its Alaska Accelerate vision, expanding its international network, launching a new loyalty program, and executing significant share repurchases. For 2026, the airline forecasts earnings per share between $3.50 and $6.50, driven by the rollout of new initiatives and a focus on harvesting previous investments.

title: Northrop Grumman's (NYSE:NOC) Q4 CY2025 Sales Top Estimates
summary: Northrop Grumman (NYSE:NOC) reported better-than-expected revenue and GAAP profit in Q4 CY2025, with sales up 9.6% year-on-year to $11.71 billion and EPS significantly beating estimates. However, the company's full-year revenue guidance came in slightly below analyst expectations. Despite mixed long-term growth trends in revenue and operating margin, the company's backlog growth and recent EPS acceleration suggest underlying demand and effective financial management.

title: Boeing (NYSE:BA) Reports Upbeat Q4 CY2025
summary: Boeing (NYSE:BA) reported strong Q4 CY2025 results, with revenue of $23.95 billion, significantly beating analyst estimates. The company also posted a non-GAAP profit of $9.92 per share, a substantial improvement from previous quarters. These results indicate a solid recovery and positive momentum for Boeing heading into the new year.

title: Boeing beats loss forecast, shares steady as one-time gain masks ongoing risks (BA:NYSE)
summary: Boeing reported fourth-quarter results that surpassed Wall Street expectations for earnings and revenue, primarily driven by a one-time gain of $9.6 billion from the sale of its Digital Aviation Solutions. Despite this, the company continues to face operational and financial challenges, including operating losses in its commercial and defense segments, negative free cash flow for the year, and increased debt due to the Spirit AeroSystems acquisition. However, a record backlog of $682 billion suggests strong long-term demand for its aircraft.

title: Boeing (NYSE:BA) Reports Q4 Revenue Beat but Wider-Than-Expected Loss
summary: Boeing (NYSE:BA) reported a significant revenue beat in its Q4 2025 earnings, with $23.95 billion, a 57.1% year-over-year increase, surpassing analyst estimates. However, the company posted a wider-than-expected non-GAAP loss per share of ($1.91) compared to a forecast of ($0.40). The market reacted cautiously, with the stock down in pre-market trading, indicating investor focus on the earnings miss despite strong top-line growth and the acquisition of Spirit AeroSystems.

title: Ryanair Holdings plc (NASDAQ:RYAAY) Q3 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: Ryanair Holdings plc reported a Q3 2026 profit after tax of EUR 115 million, pre-exceptional, with traffic up 6% and fares up 4%. The company also addressed an "utterly baseless" EUR 256 million Italian AGCM fine, for which they've made a 33% provision, confident it will be overturned. Ryanair updated its FY '26 traffic guidance to 208 million passengers and provided a full-year profit after tax guidance of EUR 2.13 billion to EUR 2.23 billion, pre-exceptionals, citing strong demand and earlier Boeing deliveries.

title: Susquehanna raises Boeing (BA) PT citing bullish commercial aerospace and defense outlook
summary: Susquehanna has increased its price target for Boeing (BA) to $260 from $220, maintaining a 'Positive' rating on the stock. The firm's optimistic outlook is driven by a strong commercial aerospace environment, expectations of increasing defense spending, and Boeing's improving free cash flow. This positive revision reflects confidence in Boeing's market position and financial trajectory.

title: Maris-Tech Wins Follow-On Order as Intelligence-Gathering Tech Moves to Deployment
summary: Maris-Tech (MTEK) has secured a follow-on order for intelligence-gathering products from an existing customer, transitioning from a successful development phase to full operational deployment. This order, for deliveries in Q2 2026, reinforces the company's position in defense and intelligence edge AI solutions. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates MTEK as Neutral due to financial performance challenges and potential bearish momentum.

title: Phoenix Financial Ltd. Lowers Stock Position in The Boeing Company $BA

However, the company faces near-term risks, including missing Q4 earnings estimates and a regulatory report on lavatory design.

title: Elbit gets $183 million munitions deal from Israeli Defence Ministry
summary: Israel's Defence Ministry has signed a multi-year agreement worth $183 million with Elbit Systems for air munitions. Defence Minister Israel Katz highlighted the vital role of Israeli defence industries in achieving operational superiority across various fronts.

title: NYSE Content Advisory: Pre-Market update + Block Exceeds $200 Billion in Credit Provided to Its Users

This deal strengthens ParaZero's position as a supplier of counter-UAS systems to NATO-aligned defense entities, reflecting increased demand for drone interception technologies. title: Y Intercept Hong Kong Ltd Makes New $2.92 Million Investment in Ryanair Holdings PLC $RYAAY
summary: Y Intercept Hong Kong Ltd has made a significant new investment of $2.92 million in Ryanair Holdings PLC, acquiring 48,489 shares in Q3, bringing institutional ownership to 43.66%. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for Ryanair with an average target price of $76.00, and the company recently declared a special dividend.

title: American Airlines reports fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results
summary: American Airlines (NASDAQ: AAL) reported record fourth-quarter revenue of $14.0 billion and full-year revenue of $54.6 billion for 2025, despite a $325 million impact from a government shutdown. The company forecasts adjusted EPS of $1.70 to $2.70 and free cash flow of over $2 billion for full-year 2026, while expecting a first-quarter 2026 adjusted loss per diluted share of ($0.10) to ($0.50) due to significant winter storm disruptions. American Airlines is focused on enhancing customer experience, expanding its network and fleet, deepening loyalty programs, and improving revenue management, aiming for significant upside in 2026 and beyond.

title: Boeing BBJ ‘Freedom Plane’ Becomes Flying Archives Exhibit
summary: Boeing’s VIP-configured 737-700, dubbed the "Freedom Plane," is now serving as a flying exhibit for the National Archives Foundation. This 26-year-old Boeing Business Jet (BBJ1) will tour the U.S., showcasing historic founding documents. The initiative aims to bring important historical artifacts to a wider audience across the country.

title: Springfield gets new beyond-visual-line-of-sight drone research facility
summary: AeroVironment and CAL Analytics have opened a new Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS) airspace management center in Springfield, Ohio, at the National Advanced Air Mobility Center of Excellence. This facility, developed in agreement with the Air Force Research Laboratory (AFRL) and the Ohio Department of Transportation, allows military and civilian drones to operate safely beyond a pilot's visual range using existing FAA ground radar feeds. The technology, called "AV_Halo COMMAND," provides continuous situational awareness and decision support for predictable BVLOS operations, enhancing Ohio's capability for drone research and testing.

title: Earnings call transcript: Crane Co Q4 2025 results miss revenue forecasts
summary: Crane Co reported its Q4 2025 earnings, with adjusted EPS matching expectations at $1.53, but revenue missed forecasts significantly at $581 million versus a projected $624.82 million. This led to a 9.42% drop in the company's stock price in premarket trading. Despite the revenue miss, the company showed strong performance in 2025 with a 24% increase in full-year adjusted EPS and completed four strategic acquisitions, with an optimistic outlook and guidance for 2026.

title: Ryanair Holdings PLC (NASDAQ:RYAAY) Profit Falls
summary: Ryanair Holdings PLC reported a sharp decline in third-quarter profit to €30 million, primarily due to an €85 million charge from a fine imposed by Italy’s competition authority. Despite this, the airline saw increased revenue, passenger numbers, and average fares, reaffirming its full-year guidance for pre-exceptional profit. The company's stock fell by 1.45% following the earnings release.

title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Climb Despite Surging Shutdown Odds and Trump’s Canada Tariff Threat
summary: Despite looming concerns over a government shutdown and former President Trump's threat of tariffs on Canadian imports, both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) concluded Monday's trading session with gains. The market also reacted to strong U.S. durable goods orders for November, which exceeded expectations, alongside anticipations for the upcoming Federal Open Market Committee meeting. The positive market performance suggests investors are looking past immediate political and trade uncertainties.

title: Form ARS Construction Partners, For: Sep 30
summary: Construction Partners, Inc. (ROAD) filed its annual report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended September 30, 2025. The report details significant growth in revenues and gross profit, driven by strategic acquisitions and strong demand in existing markets. Financial highlights include increased net income, higher adjusted EBITDA, and details on recent acquisitions and debt financing.

title: Earnings call transcript: Crane Co Q4 2025 results miss revenue forecasts
summary: Crane Co reported its Q4 2025 earnings, with adjusted EPS matching expectations at $1.53, but revenue missed forecasts by 7.01%, totaling $581 million. This revenue shortfall caused Crane's stock to drop 9.42% in premarket trading. The company provided 2026 adjusted EPS guidance of $6.55 to $6.75, along with updates on strategic acquisitions and leadership transition.

title: Boeing Earnings Trounce Expectations. The Stock Finally Turns Higher.
summary: Boeing reported earnings that significantly exceeded expectations, bolstered by an asset sale gain. Despite an initial dip on Tuesday, the stock reversed course and trended higher by midday, influenced by positive delivery projections. Year-to-date, Boeing's stock performance had already outpaced the S&P 500.

title: Boeing, Technion advance sustainable aviation fuel project
summary: Boeing and the Technion-Israel Institute of Technology are moving their joint sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) initiative into the development phase, focusing on cost-competitive "electrofuel" from green hydrogen and captured carbon dioxide. The partnership, launched in 2023, aims to reduce aviation emissions, enhance energy security, and foster innovation within Israel. Despite challenges, including the October 7th attacks, the project has continued, with Boeing affirming SAF's potential to significantly cut CO2 emissions.

title: Triumph Financial Q4 Results Highlight Payments Momentum, Valuation Risks
summary: Triumph Financial (NASDAQ: TFIN) reported strong Q4 2025 results, significantly exceeding Wall Street estimates, driven by efficiency gains and a strategic pivot towards its "Triumph Network" payments platform. The company saw substantial margin expansion in its core payments segment and provided a bullish outlook for 2026, targeting a transition to a "harvest" phase with increased EBITDA margins. However, analysts noted valuation concerns given the stock's high price-to-earnings ratio and reliance on non-recurring gains for the quarter, alongside ongoing challenges in the broader freight industry.

title: NASA, GE Test Hybrid Engine for Next Decade of Flight
summary: NASA and GE Aerospace have completed initial ground testing of a hybrid-electric engine demonstrator designed for single-aisle aircraft, achieving a significant milestone in NASA's Hybrid Thermally Efficient Core (HyTEC) project. This engine, a modified GE Passport, aims to deliver 10% fuel savings by strategically utilizing electric power to assist the gas turbine. The successful test advances the technology towards potential commercial aviation integration by the 2030s, underscoring GE's broader hybrid-electric ambitions, including its RISE project targeting 20% fuel savings and applications in military and advanced air mobility.

title: Should You Invest in the First Trust Indxx Aerospace & Defense ETF (MISL)?
summary: The First Trust Indxx Aerospace & Defense ETF (MISL), launched in October 2022, offers investors broad exposure to the Industrials - Aerospace & Defense sector. With over $1.6 billion in assets and an expense ratio of 0.6%, it tracks the INDXX US AEROSPACE & DEFENSE INDEX. The ETF has shown strong performance, gaining 15.84% year-to-date and 53.66% over the last year, holding a Zacks ETF Rank of 3 (Hold).

title: Boeing's Q4 Loss Wider Than Estimates, Revenues Increase Y/Y
summary: Boeing reported a wider-than-expected adjusted loss of $1.91 per share in Q4 2025, though this was an improvement from the previous year. The company's revenues significantly increased year-over-year to $23.95 billion, surpassing analyst estimates, driven by strong growth in its commercial airplane segment. Boeing also ended 2025 with a substantial backlog of $682.2 billion and improved its financial condition with positive net cash from operating activities.

title: Boeing targets 500 737 deliveries as inventory cushion runs out (BA:NYSE)
summary: Boeing aims to deliver approximately 500 of its 737 aircraft this year as production increases. Executives have warned that the inventory cushion from previous years, which had boosted past deliveries, has now been depleted and will no longer contribute to the delivery numbers.

title: Boeing reports 1st annual profit since 2018
summary: Boeing has reported its first annual profit since 2018, totaling US$1.9 billion for 2025, primarily driven by a significant asset sale of US$10.6 billion in aviation software. The company is making progress in increasing plane production, expecting further increases for its 737 MAX and 787 Dreamliner programs in 2026, despite a previous crisis involving a January 2024 Alaska Airlines incident. CEO Kelly Ortberg anticipates free cash flow of US$1 billion to US$3 billion in 2026 as Boeing works towards a medium-term objective of US$10 billion.

title: Boeing FY2025: Company posts small profit on Services division; BCA still losing money
summary: Boeing reported its full-year 2025 results, showing a small overall profit driven by its Global Services (BGS) division and the $10.6 billion sale of part of its business. While Boeing Commercial Aircraft (BCA) continues to operate at a loss, its losses narrowed, and Boeing Defense, Space and Security (BDS) was near break-even. The company is focusing on stabilizing production rates and addressing significant debt and ongoing certification challenges for various aircraft programs like the 737 MAX 7 and 10 variants and the 777X.

title: Boeing Earnings Trounce Expectations. The Stock Is All Over the Place.
summary: Boeing (BA) reported earnings that significantly exceeded expectations, causing volatility in its stock price. Despite the strong earnings, the stock was up 16% year-to-date, outperforming the S&P 500 by a considerable margin. The article details the market's reaction to Boeing's financial performance.

title: Boeing Takes First KC-46 Charge Since 2024
summary: Boeing has announced a new $565 million charge on its KC-46A Pegasus tanker contract, marking its first loss on the program since October 2024. This charge is attributed to increased costs for the 767 airliner that forms the tanker's basis, and greater engineering support efforts. The company has now accumulated over $7 billion in losses on the fixed-price KC-46 contract, which CEO Kelly Ortberg described as a "bad contract for the last decade."

title: GE completes hybrid-electric turbofan ground test with NASA support
summary: GE Aerospace has successfully completed ground testing of a hybrid-electric turbofan engine system with support from NASA. This accomplishment signifies a significant step towards integrating electric propulsion into commercial aviation, particularly for future single-aisle aircraft. The tests validated the ability to extract, transfer, and reinject electrical power within the engine while maintaining stable operation, contributing to CFM International’s RISE program for fuel efficiency reduction.

title: Boeing looks to build on momentum at SC Dreamliner plant
summary: Boeing is stabilizing and expanding production at its 787 Dreamliner plant in North Charleston, increasing monthly output from five to eight jets, with plans to reach ten. The company also announced a $1 billion expansion to add a second assembly building and 1,000 jobs. This news follows strong financial performance in the fourth quarter, exceeding Wall Street expectations, and a significant reduction in rework hours for the Dreamliner program.

title: Delta Was Not Done After This Month’s Boeing Order — Now It’s Buying Airbus A330s And A350s Too
summary: Delta Air Lines has announced a new order for 16 Airbus A330-900s and 15 Airbus A350-900s, with options for 20 more widebody aircraft. This follows a recent order for Boeing 787-10s, indicating Delta's continued expansion of its fleet. The new Airbus planes will help fill delivery gaps and provide greater range for long-haul routes, with deliveries starting in 2029.

title: Check-Cap Regains Nasdaq Compliance, Paving Way for MBody AI Merger
summary: Check-Cap (MBAI) has regained compliance with Nasdaq's minimum shareholders' equity requirement, an important step for its planned merger with MBody AI. The merger, which received approximately 98% shareholder approval in November 2025, will transform Check-Cap into a Nasdaq-listed, embodied AI-focused platform. Despite compliance, Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, rates MBAI as "Underperform" due to significant financial challenges and lack of revenue, despite a strong equity position.

title: Boeing takes $565m hit on KC-46 tanker
summary: Boeing incurred a $565 million loss on its Air Force KC-46 tanker program in the last quarter of 2025, pushing total losses on the project to approximately $8 billion. This loss significantly impacted Boeing's defense and space business, leading to a $507 million loss for the quarter and a negative operating margin for the year. Despite the financial strain attributed to rising supply-chain costs and production support for the 767-based aircraft, Boeing executives expressed optimism for future contracts, planning to deliver more tankers in 2026 after renegotiating terms.

title: Graco rises after Q4 sales and profit growth, posts record full-year results, and has a cautious 2026 outlook
summary: Graco Inc. (NYSE: GGG) reported strong Q4 2025 results with a 8.1% increase in net sales to $593.2 million and adjusted EPS of $0.77, meeting expectations. The company also achieved record full-year 2025 results, with net sales of $2.237 billion and diluted EPS of $3.08. Despite this growth, Graco provided a cautious outlook for 2026, projecting low single-digit organic growth.

title: General Dynamics Corp.
summary: General Dynamics Corp. is a major American defense contractor headquartered in Falls Church, Va. The company was founded in 1899 as the Electric Boat Company, known for building the first submarine for the U.S. Navy. Over time, General Dynamics diversified into various defense industries, producing military aircraft, armored vehicles, and advanced electronics, and after the Cold War, reorganized into Marine Group, Combat Systems Group, and Information Systems and Technology divisions.

title: PACCAR Posts Record Highs in Services Segments Amid Softer 2025 Truck Market
summary: PACCAR Inc. reported its 87th consecutive year of profitability in 2025, driven by record performances in its parts and financial services divisions, despite a softer truck market and a one-time legal charge. The company achieved its fourth-highest annual profit in its 120-year history and expects growth in 2026, driven by an improving market outlook and strategic investments in future technologies and sustainability. PACCAR also maintained a strong market share in the U.S. and Canada Class 8 market and was recognized for its environmental transparency and support for women in transportation.

title: Another Setback? Boeing Identifies New Engine Durability Issue On The 777X
summary: Boeing has identified a new engine durability issue with the 777-9's General Electric GE9X engines. While this represents another setback for the already delayed program, it is not expected to further push back the first delivery, currently slated for early 2027. Boeing is working with General Electric to resolve the problem and continues to focus on certifying the 777-9, 737 MAX 7, and 737 MAX 10.

title: What To Expect From International Paper’s (IP) Q4 Earnings
summary: International Paper (IP) is set to report its Q4 earnings on Thursday morning. The company analysts expect revenue to grow 28.7% year over year to $5.89 billion, with adjusted earnings of $0.25 per share. The article highlights past revenue misses and provides context from peers' Q4 results, noting positive investor sentiment in the industrials segment.

title: Diabetes implant sensor maker Senseonics heads to BTIG MedTech event
summary: Senseonics Holdings, Inc. announced its management will participate in the BTIG 13th Annual MedTech, Digital Health, Life Science & Diagnostic Tools Conference on February 11, 2026, in Snowbird, Utah. The company, known for its long-term implantable continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) systems, expects to discuss its technology and business updates. Despite this neutral conference news, the stock declined by 7.21% on the day of publication, which analysts suggest may be due to investor caution regarding potential future dilution from an active S-3 shelf registration.

title: Boeing and Ben-Gurion University launch aviation cybersecurity research center in Israel
summary: Boeing and Ben-Gurion University of the Negev have partnered to establish a new cybersecurity research center in Israel. This $10 million, five-year collaboration aims to enhance the cybersecurity of next-generation aviation and space systems, focusing on secure, autonomous, and connected aerospace technologies. The initiative seeks to advance innovative security architectures and resilient platforms for future aviation, strengthening Israel's role as a technological hub.

title: Adani, Embraer sign pact to make aircraft in India
summary: India's Adani Group and Brazil's Embraer SA have signed a memorandum of understanding to explore a partnership to manufacture regional transport aircraft in India. This venture marks Adani's entry into commercial aviation manufacturing, leveraging its existing airport and defense/aerospace businesses. The collaboration aims to boost India's aircraft manufacturing capabilities and expand Embraer's presence in the Indian market.

title: Elevance Health Inc. Profit Climbs In Q4
summary: Elevance Health Inc. (ELV) reported a significant increase in its fourth-quarter profit, with earnings climbing to $547 million, or $2.47 per share, compared to $418 million, or $1.81 per share, in the prior year. Adjusted earnings for the period were $739 million, or $3.33 per share. The company also saw its revenue rise by 9.5% to $49.747 billion from $45.442 billion last year.

title: The Boeing Company (BA) Strengthens Global Aerospace Leadership With Dreamliner Production and Next-Gen Aircraft Programs
summary: The Boeing Company is strengthening its global aerospace leadership through increased 787 Dreamliner production and progress on next-generation aircraft programs like the 737 MAX 7 and MAX 10. Analysts at UBS reiterated a Buy rating, citing positive trends such as a significant rise in Dreamlifter flights, indicating higher production rates. The company is actively expanding its global presence, securing orders like the 9 Dreamliners from Ethiopian Airlines, while also working to certify its new 737 MAX variants with the FAA.

title: Boeing Confirms New GE9X Engine Issue on 777X
summary: Boeing has identified a new durability issue with the GE Aerospace GE9X engines on its 777-9 aircraft, adding another challenge to the long-delayed program. Despite this, the company maintains its target for the first delivery in 2027 remains unchanged, with certification flight testing continuing. This issue follows several prior technical setbacks for the 777-9 program, even as Boeing reported strong financial results for 2025.

title: Delta Airlines Settles on GE Aerospace (GE) GEnx Engines amid Tests on Hypersonic Missiles
summary: Delta Air Lines has chosen GE Aerospace's GEnx engines to power its 30 new Boeing 787-10 aircraft, including spare engines and long-term service support. This contract follows GE Aerospace's successful engine tests with Lockheed Martin for hypersonic missiles, indicating the company's strong position in aerospace propulsion technology. The GEnx engine, with over 70 million g-hours of operation since 2011, powers nearly two-thirds of all 787 aircraft.

title: The Boeing Company (NYSE:BA) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: The Boeing Company reported its Q4 2025 earnings, beating expectations with a reported EPS of $9.92. The company highlighted significant progress in stabilizing its business, increasing commercial production, and achieving record-breaking backlogs. Despite a charge on the KC-46A tanker program, Boeing is focused on elevating performance in 2026 and aims for $10 billion in free cash flow, supported by production ramps and strategic investments.

title: Boeing’s Stepped-Up Plane Deliveries Lift Sales
summary: Boeing reported a significant increase in sales, up 57% to $23.95 billion, driven by a surge in commercial jet deliveries to their highest level since 2018. The company's stepped-up production and deliveries are helping to get its business back on track, despite ongoing pressures, with its overall order backlog reaching a record $682 billion.

title: Textron Q4 Earnings Miss Estimates, Revenues Increase Y/Y
summary: Textron Inc. (TXT) reported Q4 2025 adjusted earnings of $1.73 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.74, but revenues increased by 15.7% year-over-year to $4.18 billion, beating estimates. The aerospace and defense company experienced revenue growth in its Textron Aviation and Bell segments, while providing 2026 revenue guidance of approximately $15.5 billion and adjusted earnings between $6.40-$6.60 per share, which is below the Zacks Consensus Estimate.

title: As Adani Group, Embraer Announce Deal, Boeing Says Its Focus Is "Long Term"
summary: As the Adani Group and Embraer announce a new collaboration for regional aircraft manufacturing in India, Boeing India President Salil Gupte emphasized Boeing's long-term approach to the Indian market. Gupte stated Boeing's focus is on supporting customer growth, expanding spare parts capabilities and services, and increasing sourcing from India as global production rates rise. Unlike rivals forming exclusive alliances, Boeing sees its engagement in India through broader industrialization and existing joint ventures, aiming for "Make in India for the globe" rather than just for India.

title: Jim Cramer Insists Market is Wrong About GE Aerospace (GE)
summary: Jim Cramer believes the market is incorrect in its assessment of GE Aerospace (GE), suggesting its recent dip is a buying opportunity. This sentiment is echoed by RBC Capital, which maintains an Outperform rating. GE Aerospace has shown strong growth in orders, revenue, and operating profit, particularly in its Commercial Engines & Services segment, leading to raised full-year guidance for 2025.

title: United Parcel Service Inc (NYSE:UPS) Beats Market Expectations
summary: United Parcel Service Inc (NYSE:UPS) reported Q4 FY25 results that beat market expectations, despite a 1.13% stock dip. The company announced adjusted earnings per share of $2.38 and revenue of $24.5 billion, surpassing analyst estimates. UPS also approved a Q1 2026 dividend of $1.64 per share and provided a full-year 2026 revenue outlook of approximately $89.7 billion.

title: Textron Q4 Earnings Miss Estimates, Revenues Increase Y/Y
summary: Textron Inc. (TXT) reported its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings, with adjusted EPS missing analyst estimates at $1.73 per share, despite a 29.1% increase year-over-year. The company's total revenues, however, surpassed expectations, rising 15.7% to $4.18 billion. Textron also provided its 2026 guidance, projecting revenues of approximately $15.5 billion and adjusted earnings in the range of $6.40-$6.60 per share, which is below the Zacks Consensus Estimate.

title: Boeing reports $565M loss on KC-46 as firm looks forward to repricing
summary: Boeing reported a $565 million loss on its KC-46 Pegasus refueling tanker program in the fourth quarter of 2025, adding to billions in previous losses. Despite the setback, the company sees encouraging operational performance and aims to reprice future KC-46 contracts to ensure profitability, especially as the Air Force plans to purchase more units on a sole-source basis. Boeing attributes the loss mainly to higher supply chain and production support costs, and is investing in quality improvements at its Everett facility.

title: Northrop Grumman Flashed a Buy Signal-And the Market Backed It Up
summary: Northrop Grumman's stock (NOC) displayed a strong buy signal by breaking out to new highs and confirming the move after its Q4 2025 earnings and 2026 guidance. Despite an initial dip, the stock recovered, reinforcing a new support level and setting a trajectory for continued growth, potentially reaching $777. The company's solid Q4 performance, particularly in Aeronautics and Space, along with a robust free cash flow, dividend yield, and aggressive share buybacks, are driving momentum, further supported by significant institutional investment and a strong backlog.

title: FedEx plans MD-11 cargo plane return by May 31 following UPS accident
summary: FedEx announced its intent to return grounded MD-11 cargo planes to service by May 31, collaborating with Boeing and the FAA. This follows the fatal crash of a UPS MD-11 in November, which prompted UPS to accelerate the retirement of its MD-11 fleet. The crash, which killed 15 people, was linked to a cracked part identified in a Boeing service letter over a decade prior.

title: SOUTHWEST AIRLINES REPORTS FOURTH QUARTER AND FULL YEAR 2025 RESULTS; EXPECTS STRONG 2026 FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE FROM BUSINESS TRANSFORMATION
summary: Southwest Airlines reported its fourth quarter and full-year 2025 financial results, with a net income of $441 million for the year and an adjusted EPS of $0.93. The company provided optimistic 2026 adjusted EPS guidance of at least $4.00, representing a more than 300% increase over 2025, driven by significant business transformations implemented in 2025. These transformations include bag fees, basic economy fares, assigned seating, and improved operational reliability, which also contributed to Southwest being ranked #1 in The Wall Street Journal's Best U.S. Airlines of 2025.

title: AAR Expands MRO Services in Oklahoma City with New Maintenance Bays for 737 Aircraft
summary: AAR CORP has completed a significant expansion of its Airframe MRO facility in Oklahoma City, adding over 80,000 square feet and three new maintenance bays for Boeing 737 aircraft. This expansion aims to meet increasing demand for MRO services and strengthens AAR's long-standing partnership with Alaska Airlines, which will induct more aircraft into the facility. The initiative also created 200 new jobs and includes plans for modernizing maintenance with digitization and advanced technologies.

title: Hexcel's (NYSE:HXL) Q4 CY2025 Sales Top Estimates, Stock Soars
summary: Hexcel (NYSE:HXL) reported Q4 CY2025 results that exceeded revenue expectations, with sales growing 3.7% year-on-year to $491.3 million, causing its stock to rise. While full-year revenue guidance was slightly below analysts' estimates, non-GAAP profit per share beat consensus. The company cited positive trends in commercial aircraft production and increasing defense spending as drivers for a stronger outlook in 2026.

title: Southwest Airlines Stock Outlook: Is Wall Street Bullish or Bearish?
summary: Southwest Airlines (LUV) has seen its stock outperform the broader market over the past year, driven by investor optimism regarding its strategic transformation, including new revenue initiatives and strong demand. Analysts expect a significant rise in EPS for fiscal year 2026, with the consensus being a "Hold" rating that shows a more bullish sentiment than a month ago. Jefferies recently raised its price target for LUV, citing fleet retrofits and earnings potential from new seating options.

title: A Look At General Electric’s Valuation After Strong 2025 Results And Growing Aerospace And Vernova Order Momentum
summary: General Electric (GE) reported strong 2025 full-year results with US$45.9b revenue and US$8.7b net income, yet its shares saw a short-term decline. Despite this pullback, the stock is considered 13.9% undervalued with a fair value of $339.69, driven by significant productivity gains and increased free cash flow. The article suggests that while longer-term momentum remains strong, with a 1-year total shareholder return of 47.74%, investors should consider the P/E ratio and other risks before making investment decisions.

title: A Look At General Electric’s Valuation After Strong 2025 Results And Growing Aerospace And Vernova Order Momentum
summary: General Electric (GE) reported strong full-year 2025 results with significant revenue and net income, alongside new contracts, yet experienced a short-term share price decline. The P/E ratio aligns with GE's fair ratio but is below the industry average, indicating recognized quality with potential for limited safety margin if sentiment shifts.

title: The Bull Case For RTX (RTX) Could Change Following Record Backlog And 2026 Growth Guidance - Learn Why
summary: RTX Corporation reported strong 2025 results with US$88,603 million in sales and US$6,732 million in net income, and provided optimistic 2026 guidance including 5% to 6% organic sales growth. While the stock has risen and is potentially undervalued, investors are cautioned about increased expectations and the potential impact of execution missteps.

title: KC-46 Pegasus Adds $565M Loss to Boeing’s Defense Balance Sheet
summary: Boeing reported a $565 million loss for its KC-46 Pegasus tanker program in Q4 2025, bringing the total accumulated losses to over $8 billion. This setback is attributed to rising production and supply chain costs, particularly concerning the 767-based airframe. Despite the financial challenges, the US Air Force continues to order KC-46 aircraft due to improving operational performance and its strategic role until a next-generation tanker is available.

title: Earnings call transcript: Hexcel Q4 2025 beats expectations, stock rises
summary: Hexcel Corporation exceeded analyst expectations in Q4 2025, reporting an EPS of $0.52 against a forecast of $0.49 and revenue of $491 million compared to an anticipated $480.16 million. title: Why Investors Shouldn't Be Surprised By Vista Energy, S.A.B. de C.V.'s (BMV:VISTAA) Low P/E
summary: Vista Energy, S.A.B. de C.V. (BMV:VISTAA) has a low price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio of 8.5x, significantly lower than the Mexican market average. The low P/E reflects investor sentiment that improvements in earnings are not substantial enough to warrant a higher valuation, placing pressure on the share price given the weak outlook.

title: eVTOL Aircraft Market Set for Explosive Growth to USD 5,664.42 Billion by 2032, Led by North America's 41.2% Market Share | Key Players - Archer Aviation Inc., Joby Aviation, Vertical Aerospace
summary: The global eVTOL aircraft market is projected to grow significantly from USD 596.25 million in 2024 to USD 5,664.42 billion by 2032, driven by urbanization, demand for sustainable urban air mobility, and technological advancements. North America is expected to hold the largest market share at 42%, while Asia Pacific is anticipated to be the fastest-growing region. Key players like Joby Aviation and Archer Aviation are making strides in certification and commercialization, supported by regulatory frameworks and investments.

title: Biotech firm InflaRx lines up two investor talks in February
summary: InflaRx (Nasdaq: IFRX) has announced its participation in two investor conferences in February 2026: the Guggenheim Biotech Summit and the Oppenheimer Healthcare Life Sciences Conference. The company will offer live presentations and one-on-one meetings to investors, focusing on its lead programs izicopan and vilobelimab. A fireside chat at Guggenheim is scheduled for February 11, 2026, and a virtual presentation at Oppenheimer for February 25, 2026.

title: Inside the Florida laser lab showing Congress how to fight drones
summary: Laser Photonics (NASDAQ: LASE) hosted representatives from the office of Florida Congressman Cory Mills to showcase its laser systems for defense and industrial applications. The visit highlighted solutions like the CleanTech handheld laser cleaning systems for naval maintenance, the Bulk-to-Shape additive manufacturing technology, and the LSAD anti-drone system. This engagement underscores the company's focus on defense readiness, industrial resilience, and workforce development, reinforcing recent trends in its company history involving defense exposure and commercial orders.

title: Air India Orders 30 Boeing 737 MAX Jets to Expand Single-Aisle Fleet
summary: Air India has ordered 30 additional fuel-efficient Boeing 737 MAX jets, including 20 737-8 and 10 737-10 airplanes, to expand its single-aisle fleet for domestic and regional growth. This order brings Air India's total Boeing order book to nearly 200 airplanes and supports its strategy to meet rising travel demand and upgrade its fleet over the coming years. The airline will utilize the 737-8s for high-frequency routes and the larger 737-10s for increased passenger capacity with low operating costs.

title: Parker-Hannifin raises annual profit forecast on aerospace parts demand
summary: Parker-Hannifin has increased its annual profit forecast, driven by strong demand for aerospace parts. The company's revised outlook reflects positive performance within this sector.

title: Ryanair Holdings PLC $RYAAY Shares Sold by Ruffer LLP

Analyst sentiment toward the stock remains positive, with a consensus "Buy" rating and a price target of $73.50.

title: Why Is Southwest Airlines Stock Rising? Q4 Results and Major Business Shift
summary: Southwest Airlines (NYSE: LUV) shares surged over 12% following its Q4 2025 earnings report, driven by a major business model transformation and significantly higher-than-expected 2026 EPS guidance of at least $4.00. The airline is implementing assigned seating, baggage fees, and new fare options, which it expects to result in substantial profit growth. Despite a $30-40 million impact from Winter Storm Fern, the company delivered strong Q4 results and returned $2.9 billion to shareholders in 2025.

title: L3Harris Q4 Earnings Surpass Estimates, Revenues Rise Y/Y
summary: L3Harris Technologies, Inc. (LHX) reported fourth-quarter 2025 adjusted earnings of $2.86 per share, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $2.76, while total revenues of $5.65 billion missed expectations but rose 2.3% year-over-year. The company provided its 2026 guidance, expecting revenues between $23-23.5 billion and adjusted earnings between $11.30-$11.50 per share, with an adjusted free cash flow of around $3 billion. title: Which Airline Has The Largest Boeing 747 Fleet In 2026?
summary: Atlas Air holds the distinction of being the world's largest operator of the Boeing 747, managing approximately 15% of the remaining operational Jumbo jets. The company, which specializes in cargo, leasing, and charter services, has a diverse fleet of 65 Boeing 747s, including both older 747-400s and newer 747-8 variants. Despite the 747's production ceasing in 2023, Atlas Air continues to expand its fleet by acquiring second-hand aircraft, solidifying its dominant position in operating the iconic "Queen of the Skies."

title: Hexcel: fourth quarter 2025 results exceed those of fourth quarter 2024
source: JEC - Connecting the World with Composites
summary: Hexcel Corporation reported higher net sales for Q4 2025 compared to Q4 2024, despite 2025 being a challenging year due to delays in commercial aircraft production. While full-year 2025 revenue saw a slight decrease from 2024, the company observed positive trends in Q4 2025, suggesting a stronger recovery in 2026, driven by increasing commercial aircraft build rates and defense spending. Hexcel anticipates 8% sales growth and 25% adjusted EPS growth at the midpoint for 2026, supported by operating leverage and cost control.

title: Earnings call transcript: Southwest Airlines Q4 2025 sees EPS beat, stock surges
summary: Southwest Airlines reported Q4 2025 earnings with an EPS of $0.58, exceeding the forecast of $0.57, though revenue of $7.4 billion fell short of the $7.5 billion expectation. Despite the revenue miss, the company's stock surged 6.22% in premarket trading due to strong future guidance and operational achievements, including a projected 2026 adjusted EPS of at least $4. The call highlighted successful implementation of new initiatives like assigned seating, extra legroom, and expanded online presence, driving optimism for continued growth and profitability.

title: Forget Rigetti Computing, This Established AI Giant Is a Safer Play
summary: This article argues that while quantum computing has revolutionary potential, Rigetti Computing, a pure-play quantum computing stock, is a risky investment due to its small size, limited market, and high expenses. Instead, it suggests that investors consider IBM, an established AI giant with a significant quantum computing presence, strong financials, and a dividend, as a much safer long-term investment in the emerging tech industries.

title: Air India Orders 30 Boeing 737 MAX Jets to Expand Single-Aisle Fleet
summary: Air India has placed an additional order for 30 fuel-efficient Boeing 737 MAX jets, comprising 20 737-8s and 10 737-10s. This expands their total Boeing order to nearly 200 airplanes and supports their strategy for domestic, regional, and short-haul route expansion to meet increasing travel demand in India and South Asia. The 737 MAX jets were chosen for their reliability, fuel efficiency, and capacity to carry more passengers at a lower cost.

title: HII Names Fatina Brave Vice President of Infrastructure and Sustainability at Ingalls Shipbuilding
summary: HII announced the appointment of Fatina Brave as vice president of infrastructure and sustainability at its Ingalls Shipbuilding division. Brave, a veteran and long-time HII employee, will oversee environmental, health, safety, security, facilities, and maintenance operations. She succeeds Eric Crooker, who moved to program management, and will report to Ingalls Shipbuilding President Brian Blanchette.

title: Boeing Earnings: Climbing Out of a Very Deep Hole
summary: Boeing delivered significantly more 737 and 787 jets in Q4 2025 compared to 2024, despite recording additional losses on its KC-46 tanker program. Increasing the production and delivery rate of its 737 jets is identified as the primary driver for Boeing's profitability and cash flow. Morningstar maintains a fair value estimate of $246 per share, anticipating the company will meet its free cash flow goals by 2028 if jet deliveries and certifications progress as planned.

title: Boeing Stock Falls Again. But There Is Cash Flow Potential.
summary: Boeing's stock has fallen despite delivering solid fourth-quarter numbers, dropping for two consecutive days. However, Wall Street analysts are defending the company, emphasizing its future potential for rising free cash flow. This focus on cash flow is seen as a key positive aspect for the aerospace giant.

title: Stellantis Sets May 21 Investor Day to Unveil New Strategic Plan
summary: Stellantis N.V. has announced an Investor Day for May 21, 2026, in Auburn Hills, Michigan, where it will reveal its new strategic plan. This event is crucial for the automaker to articulate its medium- to long-term direction to investors amid the ongoing transformation in the global automotive industry. Currently, analysts rate Stellantis stock as a Hold with a EUR9.00 price target.

title: Major airlines urge Congress to pay air traffic controllers if government shuts down
summary: Major U.S. airlines are urging Congress to ensure air traffic controllers are paid during any potential government shutdown, citing severe disruptions from a previous 43-day standoff. The industry group Airlines for America highlighted that past shutdowns caused flight cuts, affected millions of passengers, and resulted in significant economic losses. A House committee has already approved legislation aimed at preventing such aviation disruptions by guaranteeing payment for essential workers like air traffic controllers.

title: Boeing’s 737 MAX Delivery Pace Tells the Truth Earnings Reports Can’t Hide
summary: Boeing is struggling to meet its 737 MAX delivery targets, significantly impacting its revenue and indicating ongoing production issues. Despite a backlog of over 4,000 jets, the company is delivering only 20-25 planes monthly against a target of 38, resulting in a substantial monthly revenue loss. This slow delivery pace, attributed to quality problems and regulatory scrutiny, suggests a deeper existential crisis for Boeing, especially as competitor Airbus delivers planes on time.

title: Southwest Airlines shares soar to highest levels in years
summary: Southwest Airlines reported record revenue for both the fourth quarter and the full year, following a period of significant changes including layoffs, new products, and ending its long-standing open-seating policy. This strong financial performance has led to a surge in its share price. The airline's shares have reached their highest levels in years on the back of a big forecast for 2026.

title: American Airlines Flight Attendants Tell Me Why They’ve Lost Faith In Leadership…And Why They Hate The A321XLR
summary: Flight attendants at American Airlines express severe dissatisfaction with leadership and the design of the new A321XLR aircraft, highlighting a significant trust deficit within the company. They cite issues like narrow aisles, difficult boarding procedures, and limited galley space on the A321XLR, causing service challenges. The article suggests that a change in leadership is necessary to rebuild morale and articulate a clear vision for the airline's future, as many employees feel AA is moving in the wrong direction.

title: Hexcel Q4 Earnings Call Highlights
summary: Hexcel executives anticipate a sustained recovery in commercial aerospace, projecting higher build rates for 2026 across major Airbus and Boeing programs after a challenging 2025. The company shared its 2026 outlook, forecasting sales of $2.0–$2.1 billion, adjusted EPS of $2.10–$2.30, and free cash flow exceeding $195 million. Hexcel plans to strategically increase hiring and repay debt from its accelerated share repurchase, while also hiking its quarterly dividend by 6%.

title: Hexcel Corp (HXL): Analyst Targets Raised as 2026 Outlook Forecasts Continued Ramp
summary: Wall Street analysts have raised price targets for Hexcel Corp (HXL) following the company's positive 2026 sales guidance of $2.0 billion to $2.1 billion, signaling renewed growth. Goldman Sachs increased its target to $75.00, while UBS previously set its target at $96.00. Hexcel also announced a 6% dividend hike to $0.18 per share and has an ongoing share repurchase program, reflecting confidence in its financial outlook despite aircraft production remaining below pre-pandemic levels.

title: Global Minimum Tax And Delta Deal Reshape GE Aerospace Outlook
summary: GE Aerospace is facing increased tax expenses due to Singapore's adoption of the 15% global minimum corporate tax, which has reduced its tax benefits by $121 million. Simultaneously, the company secured a significant long-term deal with Delta Air Lines to supply GEnx engines, spare parts, and services for its Boeing 787-10 fleet, creating a multi-year revenue stream. These developments present a mixed outlook for investors, with higher tax burdens potentially impacting future earnings but the Delta deal providing long-term revenue visibility and reinforcing GE Aerospace's market position.

title: VSE acquires Precision Aviation Group in US$2bn deal
summary: VSE Corporation has agreed to acquire Precision Aviation Group (PAG) for approximately US$2.025 billion in cash and equity. This acquisition will significantly expand VSE's scale and strengthen its aviation aftermarket services, combining diverse technical expertise and proprietary repair solutions. PAG, founded in 1996, is a global provider of MRO services and supply chain solutions, expecting to generate around US$615 million in adjusted revenue for 2025.

title: AustralianSuper Pty Ltd Purchases New Shares in Ryanair Holdings PLC $RYAAY
summary: AustralianSuper Pty Ltd acquired a new stake of 251,300 shares in Ryanair Holdings PLC (NASDAQ:RYAAY) during the third quarter, valued at approximately $15.13 million. title: Adani Joins Hands With Embraer to Build Commercial Aircraft in India

This partnership aims to localize manufacturing, supply chains, and services for regional jets in India, fostering a comprehensive regional transport aircraft ecosystem and supporting the "Make in India" initiative. Both companies envision this collaboration as a significant step towards boosting India's aviation sector, elevating its position in global aerospace, and enhancing connectivity, especially in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.

title: QUALCOMM INC (NASDAQ:QCOM) Embodies Quality Investing Principles
summary: This article highlights QUALCOMM INC (NASDAQ:QCOM) as a strong example of a quality investing candidate, excelling in financial strength, profitability, and cash flow according to the "Caviar Cruise" screening method. summary: Hexcel (HXL) reported strong Q4 CY2025 results, with revenue exceeding expectations due to recovering commercial aerospace demand and effective cost management. title: Astronics vs. AAR: Which is the Stronger Aerospace Services Stock?
summary: The article analyzes Astronics (ATRO) and AAR (AIR) to determine which is a stronger aerospace services stock, given rising global aircraft deliveries and increasing demand for maintenance services. While AAR also shows solid financial results and growth in MRO and technology initiatives, Astronics is deemed to have stronger near-term outperformance potential due to its growth visibility and higher Zacks Rank.

title: Moog Inc. Reports First Quarter 2026 Record Sales and EPS and Raises Full-Year Guidance
summary: Moog Inc. reported record fiscal first-quarter 2026 results with strong execution and significant progress towards its long-term financial objectives. This performance was driven by robust demand across all segments, particularly Commercial Aircraft and Space and Defense.

summary: Carpenter Technology Corporation (NYSE:CRS) reported record earnings in Q2 2026, exceeding expectations with an EPS of $2.33. title: Inside Alaska Airlines’ $200M hub training thousands of staff
summary: Alaska Air Group (NYSE: ALK) has officially opened its new $200 million, 660,000-square-foot Global Training Center in Renton, Washington. This state-of-the-art facility will centralize training for thousands of Alaska Airlines and Hawaiian Airlines frontline employees, including pilots, flight attendants, and customer service agents. It features full-motion simulators, mock bays, VR rooms, and numerous amenities, significantly expanding the airline's training capacity and supporting its Alaska Accelerate strategic plan for growth and integration.

title: Hexcel Corporation (NYSE:HXL) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: Hexcel Corporation discusses its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings, highlighting a challenging year impacted by destocking and schedule delays but ending with positive commercial aerospace order trends. summary: Howmet Aerospace Inc. (HWM) has shown strong performance, outperforming the S&P 500 and the Invesco Aerospace & Defense ETF, largely due to its acquisition of Consolidated Aerospace Manufacturing, LLC. title: Air Force None: Why Trump's Big, Beautiful Boeings Can't Fly Yet
summary: This article details the significant delays and cost overruns plaguing Boeing's delivery of new Air Force One jets, originally promised by 2024. title: RBC Capital Raises Price Target for Hexcel (HXL) to $95 | HXL St
summary: RBC Capital has maintained its Outperform rating on Hexcel Corporation (HXL) and raised its price target from $92 to $95. title: Why The Boeing 777 Has Such A Massive Global Presence
summary: The Boeing 777 has achieved a dominant global presence in commercial aviation due to its unique blend of range, capacity, efficiency, and reliability, becoming the first twin-engine widebody designed for long-haul operations. Introduced in the mid-1990s, it enabled new nonstop intercontinental routes and quickly became a backbone aircraft for international travel. Despite facing competition, particularly from the Airbus A350, the 777 family, including the new 777X series, continues to evolve, maintaining its role as a key player in global aviation.

title: Key facts: General Dynamics reports $52.6B revenue; U.S. approves $1.7B sale; Trump decertifies Bombardier jets

However, President Trump's decertification of Bombardier Global Express jets poses a potential concern for General Dynamics due to its aerospace interests.

title: Boeing reaches labor deal with former Spirit AeroSystems white-collar workers

title: How Much Does Lockheed Martin’s F-35 Program Cost In 2026?

The article highlights that total lifecycle costs for the F-35 program, exemplified by the US projection of $2 trillion sustained until 2088, are substantial due to maintenance, support infrastructure, munitions, and an extended operational lifespan, making it difficult to compare directly with less capable 4th-generation fighters.

Despite this, other institutional investors like Balyasny Asset Management and Brown Advisory Inc. increased their holdings in the oil and gas company. Insider activity also saw SVP Jennifer Fremont Simons sell a portion of her shares, while analysts have a mixed view, with a consensus "Hold" rating and a target price of $25.00 for OII.

title: TotalEnergies inks 3.3 TWh of green power deals with Airbus
summary: TotalEnergies has signed agreements with Airbus for the supply of 3.3 TWh of green electricity. These corporate Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs) will cover Airbus's European operations in France, Germany, and the UK. The deals aim to support Airbus in its energy transition goals by providing renewable power.

title: Analysts Have Conflicting Sentiments on These Industrial Goods Companies: Boeing (BA) and Zurn Water Solutions (ZWS)
summary: Analysts are presenting conflicting sentiments on industrial goods companies Boeing (BA) and Zurn Water Solutions (ZWS). DZ BANK AG analyst Robert Czerwensky maintained a Buy rating on Boeing, while KeyBanc's Jeffrey Hammond maintained a Hold rating on Zurn Water Solutions, despite a Moderate Buy consensus for Zurn. The article provides details on analyst ratings, price targets, and potential upsides for both stocks.

title: GXO takes on shipyard logistics for Royal Navy Type 26 frigates
summary: GXO (NYSE: GXO) has renewed and expanded its six-year contract with BAE Systems in the UK to support the production of Type 26 frigates for the Royal Navy. This agreement extends their partnership into its third decade, with GXO providing warehousing, materials handling, and 4PL Control Tower services at BAE Systems' Scotstoun and Govan shipyards. This move strengthens GXO's aerospace & defence presence, particularly following its recent acquisition of Wincanton.

title: Boeing snags $2.8B contract to upgrade St. Louis-made F-15s for South Korea
summary: Boeing has secured a $2.8 billion contract from the U.S. Air Force to upgrade F-15 fighter jets manufactured in St. Louis for the South Korean government. This contract highlights Boeing's continued role in military aviation and its St. Louis operations. The announcement was made by the St. Louis Business Journal.

title: Boeing to cut 300 jobs from its defense division in February 2026: Bloomberg
summary: Boeing is reportedly planning to eliminate approximately 300 jobs from its Boeing Defense, Space & Security division in February 2026, with employee notifications expected this week. This follows previous large-scale job cuts in late 2024 and early 2025 due to financial and operational challenges, as well as delays in programs like the Space Launch System. Despite these reductions, Boeing's overall workforce numbers have remained relatively stable.

title: Why Boeing is Reshaping its Defence Supply Chain Workforce
summary: Boeing is set to reduce its defense supply chain workforce by 300 jobs, following a previous announcement in January to cut 10% of positions in this sector. This move is part of the company's efforts to address financial struggles, despite reporting a 57% year-on-year revenue increase in Q4 2025, as its defense sector still experienced a loss. Additionally, Boeing is relocating 787 Dreamliner program work from Seattle to South Carolina, impacting 250-300 workers and raising concerns from the SPEEA union.

title: Air India probes if crew followed protocols in Boeing fuel-switch incident
summary: Air India is investigating whether its crew followed protocols after a Boeing jet took off from London with a potential fuel-switch defect, only to be grounded in India. Britain's aviation authority has requested details from Air India regarding maintenance actions before the flight, giving them a week to respond to avoid regulatory action. The investigation will also question why the incident was not reported in London but only upon landing in India, especially given that fuel switches were central to a previous Air India Dreamliner crash.

title: Israel to boost CH-53K performance with package of Elbit Systems equipment updates
summary: Elbit Systems will supply command and control, avionics, and electronic warfare systems, including a directional infrared countermeasures system, for Israel's 12 Sikorsky CH-53K heavy-lift helicopters. This $130 million deal aims to enhance the cockpit, enable flight in challenging conditions, and improve pilot safety. The equipment will be integrated into the US-built helicopters to suit Israel's unique operational requirements, replacing the air force's aging CH-53 "Yasur" fleet.

title: GXO Logistics, Inc. (GXO): A Bull Case Theory
summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on GXO Logistics, Inc. that highlights the company's position as the world's largest pure-play contract logistics provider. Key points include its investment in automation, AI, and robotics to drive efficiency and margin expansion, as well as its strong revenue visibility and diversified business model. The article suggests a potential valuation of $80+ per share, building on previous bullish coverage that saw the stock appreciate by 46.64%.

title: Families of plane crash victims ask US appeals court to revive a criminal case against Boeing
summary: Families of victims from two fatal Boeing 737 Max crashes are asking a federal appeals court to reinstate a criminal conspiracy charge against the aircraft manufacturer. They argue that federal prosecutors violated their rights by not consulting them before striking a deal that allowed Boeing to avoid prosecution. Boeing contends it has taken steps to improve and paid compensation, while the government asserts it weighed families' views throughout the process.

title: Boeing to Move 787 Engineering Work
summary: Boeing plans to move approximately 300 787 Dreamliner engineering jobs from Washington state to South Carolina. This strategic relocation affects unionized engineers in Washington and aims to support Boeing's $1 billion investment to expand 787 production capacity in Charleston, South Carolina, by adding a second final assembly line. The decision to move reflects the company's commitment to meeting high global demand for the widebody aircraft, leveraging South Carolina's significantly lower rates of union participation.

title: Families of plane crash victims ask US appeals court to revive a criminal case against Boeing
summary: Thirty-one families of victims from two fatal Boeing 737 Max crashes have appealed to a federal court to reinstate a criminal case against the aerospace giant. They argue that federal prosecutors violated their rights by failing to consult them before striking a deal that allowed Boeing to avoid prosecution in exchange for fines and compensation. The families seek a trial, asserting that without one, there will not be meaningful accountability for the 346 lives lost.

title: Families of plane crash victims ask US appeals court to revive a criminal case against Boeing
summary: Families of the 346 victims from two fatal Boeing 737 Max crashes are urging a federal appeals court to reinstate criminal charges against the aircraft manufacturer. They argue that federal prosecutors violated their rights by not properly consulting them before striking a deal that allowed Boeing to avoid prosecution. Boeing contends it deeply regrets the crashes, has taken steps to improve processes, and has compensated victims' families.

title: Families of plane crash victims ask US appeals court to revive a criminal case against Boeing

This order reinforces the MH-139A's role in nuclear security and defense missions, replacing the aging UH-1N Huey fleet. The helicopter, based on the Leonardo AW139, offers significant upgrades in speed, range, and survivability for patrolling ICBM fields and supporting other critical operations.

title: Delta Air Lines Bets On Fleet Refresh To Deepen Premium International Growth
summary: Delta Air Lines has placed significant new aircraft orders from Airbus and Boeing, marking its first direct widebody purchase from Boeing since 2008. These multibillion-dollar deals aim to modernize and diversify Delta's international fleet and expand its premium capacity, aligning with the airline's strategy to focus on higher-margin international and business travel. For investors, this fleet refresh could lead to durable pricing power on key routes, though it also increases capital commitments and comes with risks like potential declines in earnings over the next three years.

title: Boeing, BGU launch Aviation Cybersecurity Research Center in Israel
summary: Ben-Gurion University of the Negev (BGU) and Boeing have partnered to establish an Aviation Cybersecurity Research Center in Israel. This multi-year, $10 million collaboration aims to advance cybersecurity capabilities for next-generation autonomous, digital, and connected aviation and aerospace systems. The center will focus on developing secure architectures, communication systems, and resilient platforms, leveraging BGU's expertise in cybersecurity and AI.

title: Boeing buys long-leased property near Everett factory
summary: Boeing has acquired a large industrial property near its Everett factory for $54 million. The company had been leasing this property for over a decade. This purchase follows Boeing leasing another significant industrial property in the same area last year.

title: Boeing to move some engineering work out of Washington state
summary: Boeing is shifting some 787 Dreamliner engineering work from Washington state to South Carolina, a move that has surprised and concerned the Society of Professional Engineering Employees in Aerospace (SPEEA) union. SPEEA, representing over 24,000 workers, is seeking answers regarding job security and advance notice, citing contractual rights. This shift comes amidst existing labor tensions and impending contract negotiations, potentially complicating future discussions between Boeing and its engineers.

title: StandardAero completes first LEAP engine performance restoration shop visit
summary: StandardAero has successfully completed its first performance restoration shop visit (PRSV) for a CFM International LEAP engine, delivering a LEAP-1A engine owned by AerCap after successful pass-off testing. This milestone indicates the growing maturity of StandardAero's LEAP maintenance program, which supports over 20 customers globally, and expands its MRO capabilities for LEAP-1A and LEAP-1B engines. The company operates as a CFM LEAP Premier MRO provider and signed its first non-airline CFM Branded Service Agreement in the Americas in March 2023.

title: 3 Stocks That May Be Undervalued By Market Estimates In February 2026

title: Broken Promises: How Southwest Is Impacted By Boeing 737 Delays
summary: Southwest Airlines, despite a positive investor response to its latest annual results, faces significant challenges due to Boeing 737 MAX delivery delays. The airline's recent 10-K SEC filing reveals that it expects to receive over 100 fewer aircraft than contractually obligated in 2026, impacting its transition to an all-MAX fleet by 2031. Specifically, delays in the 737 MAX 7 certification mean Boeing must deliver an average of one MAX 7 per week for five years to meet Southwest's retirement goals for its older 737 NGs.

The agreement, signed on January 30, 2026, involves the issuance of Class A common stock at the prevailing market price and includes a true-up mechanism to protect the investor from future dilution. The company plans to file a resale registration statement with the SEC by April 20, 2026, as it seeks to manage capital and liquidity.

title: Stratasys launches SAF PA12 qualification program for aerospace use
summary: Stratasys has launched a qualification program for SAF PA12, a nylon material designed for selective absorption fusion additive manufacturing in aerospace and industrial applications. This initiative aims to accelerate the adoption of scalable, qualified additive manufacturing by extending Stratasys' AIS advanced industrial solution package to SAF technology. The program involves a collaboration with major manufacturers and additive manufacturing service bureaus like Boeing, Northrop Grumman, and Raytheon, utilizing the NCAMP materials qualification process to ensure material performance, consistency, and traceability.

title: AerCap (AER) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript

title: Press Release: LATAM extends AeroSHARK use across 777-300ER fleet

title: Why In The World Was Boeing’s 1st Commercial Aircraft A Narrowbody Quadjet?
summary: Boeing's first commercial aircraft, the 707, was a narrowbody quadjet due to the limitations of jet engine technology in the 1950s.
